items.title: fix(server): harden gateway header-map normalization
items.content:
## Thinking Path

> - Paperclip é o control plane que orquestra agentes e seus fluxos de entrada, approval e execução
> - A trilha afetada aqui é o subsistema de access/invite accept para joins `openclaw_gateway`
> - O scan do Sourcery apontou risco de injection via bracket notation em rotas Express
> - A leitura do source mostrou que a maior parte dos usos dinâmicos em rotas era interna ou delimitada por schema, mas `server/src/routes/access.ts` ainda normalizava mapas de headers com chaves arbitrárias em objeto plain
> - Mesmo sem exploit prático comprovado, essa superfície aceitava chaves especiais sem hardening explícito
> - Esta pull request endurece a normalização de header maps para descartar chaves especiais e usar objeto sem protótipo
> - O benefício é reduzir ambiguidade de segurança e transformar um finding do scanner em defesa barata com regressão explícita

## What Changed

- Hardened `normalizeHeaderMap()` in `server/src/routes/access.ts` to build header maps with `Object.create(null)`
- Dropped unsafe special keys (`__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`) during gateway header normalization
- Added a regression test in `server/src/__tests__/invite-accept-gateway-defaults.test.ts` proving unsafe keys are discarded while valid gateway headers are preserved

## Verification

- `pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/invite-accept-gateway-defaults.test.ts server/src/__tests__/invite-accept-replay.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @paperclipai/server typecheck`

## Risks

- Low risk
- Behavior changes only for special object keys that should never be accepted as normalized gateway headers
- Legitimate header keys continue to pass through unchanged

## Model Used

- OpenAI GPT-5 via Codex CLI agent in tool-use mode, with repository inspection, shell execution, git, and GitHub workflow support

## Checklist

- [x] I have included a thinking path that traces from project context to this change
- [x] I have specified the model used (with version and capability deta

items.title: fix: 2nd-time STT failure + settings wiring + multi-provider TTS
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes the most critical voice issues reported and adds multi-provider TTS.

### Problem 1: 2nd-time STT fails with NO_MATCH (wake word still works)
**Root cause**: VoskWakeWordDetector had `if (read <= 0) continue` which became an infinite tight loop when AudioRecord was released (returns ERROR_DEAD_OBJECT = -6). The loop held the microphone, blocking subsequent STT sessions.

**Fix**:
- Break on negative read values (errors), not continue
- Try-catch around blocking read() for IllegalStateException
- Set `_isListening = false` + `isPaused = true` BEFORE releaseAudio()
- Finally block guarantees cleanup on any exit
- VoiceService: destroy + recreate wake word detector on PAUSE (OpenClaw pattern)
- Cancel pending resume jobs to prevent race conditions
- VoicePipeline mic release delay 300ms → 500ms

### Problem 2: Settings saved but not applied (6 settings)
Audit found 6 preferences that were saved to DataStore but never read at runtime:

| Setting | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| TTS_LANGUAGE | Applied via `applyTtsLanguagePreference` in VoicePipeline |
| BARGE_IN_ENABLED | Checked before stopping TTS on mic tap |
| SILENCE_TIMEOUT_MS | Applied to AndroidSttProvider |
| STT_LANGUAGE | Applied to AndroidSttProvider |
| MEDIA_BUTTON_ENABLED | Checked in MediaButtonReceiver (default off) |
| HOTWORD_ENABLED | New UI toggle, gates VoiceService.initializeWakeWord |

### Problem 3: TTS cannot be switched
Added **TtsManager** that routes to one of:
- Android system TTS (default)
- OpenAI TTS (`/v1/audio/speech`)
- ElevenLabs (`/v1/text-to-speech/{id}`)
- VOICEVOX (HTTP engine mode — user runs the engine on LAN)

Each provider reads its own config from preferences at speak time. API keys stored in EncryptedSharedPreferences. Settings UI shows provider radio + conditional fields.

### OpenClaw feature ports
- Long TTS chunking at sentence boundaries (。！？.!?) — better prosody, no timeouts
- Partial WakeLock during voice session — prevents MIUI freeze mid-conversation
-

items.title: feat: enhance OpenClaw session graph text capture and tool call/result linking
items.content:
## Summary

This PR improves the OpenClaw session graph so it captures more useful session behavior from transcript files and makes tool execution easier to inspect in the UI.

Compared with `master`, the transcript sync now extracts richer `text` and metadata from OpenClaw sessions, including tool call commands, tool result bodies, error text, paths, URLs, queries, and status. The session graph drawer also adds a linked Call / Result view so users can inspect a tool call and its matching result side by side.

## Changes

- Enhanced OpenClaw transcript scanning for session graph entries.
- Captures more useful `text` for tasks, assistant finals, tool calls, and tool results.
- Adds a Call / Result linked panel in the session graph drawer.

items.title: [Bug][Core] OpenClaw runtime fails to start on Windows (Path not found), causing Controller WS loop
items.content:
## Environment

**Platform:** Windows
**Version:** 0.1.12-nightly.20260416
**Component:** OpenClaw Runtime / Controller Orchestrator

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Launch Nexu Desktop on Windows.
2. Open the `nexu local cold-start control room` dashboard.
3. Observe the status of the **OpenClaw** and **Controller** components.

## Actual Behavior

- The **OpenClaw** component remains in a **STOPPED** state.
- The **Controller** is **RUNNING** (e.g., bound to port 50800), but is completely non-functional. It is stuck in an infinite loop: `openclaw_ws_connecting -> openclaw_ws_error`.
- The Controller stderr outputs garbled text: `ϵͳÕҲ»µ½Ö¸¶¨µÄÂ·¾¶`, which is GBK encoding for **"系统找不到指定的路径"** (The system cannot find the path specified).
- `openclaw-2026-04-16.log` shows: `WARNING: could not find openclaw global installation, symlink not created`.

## Expected Behavior

- The orchestrator should correctly resolve the executable paths for the OpenClaw runtime unit on Windows (handling `\\` vs `/` and spaces in paths correctly).
- If OpenClaw fails to start, the Controller should not silently hang in a WS loop, but rather surface a clear "Engine missing/failed to start" error to the frontend UI.

## Impact

**Severity:** P0 (Critical)
- Core engine fails to start on Windows.
- Completely blocks users from using the agent.
- Garbled GBK logs make debugging extremely difficult for average users.

## Root Cause Speculation

1. **Path Resolution Bug on Windows:** The spawn/exec command for OpenClaw in the orchestrator is likely passing an invalid path (e.g., missing quotes around `C:\Users\Amy\AppData\Local\Programs\...` or incorrect binary extension like missing `.exe` or `.cmd`).
2. **Antivirus/Defender Isolation:** Windows Defender might have quarantined the OpenClaw binary during the nightly update.
3. **Missing Symlink Logic:** The script relies on a global symlink that fails to create on Windows due to strict permission requirements (requires Admin for symlinks on older Wi

items.title: feat(nodes): add file_pull/file_push actions for bidirectional file transfer
items.content:
## Summary

Implements bidirectional file transfer between gateway and paired node hosts via the `nodes` agent tool. This removes the current friction of needing rsync/SSH for every file operation.

**Layer 1** — RPC contract: `file.read` (node→gateway) and `file.write` (gateway→node) commands, following the same base64-in-JSON pattern as `camera.snap`.

**Layer 2** — Agent tool: two new `nodes` actions: `action=file_pull` and `action=file_push`.

## Changes

### New files
- `src/cli/nodes-file.ts` — `FileReadPayload`/`FileWriteResponse` types, `parseFileReadPayload`, `fileTempPath`, `writeFilePayloadToFile` helpers (modeled after `nodes-camera.ts`)
- `src/cli/nodes-file.test.ts` — 7 unit tests for payload parsers and helpers

### Modified files
- `src/infra/node-commands.ts` — `NODE_FILE_READ_COMMAND`, `NODE_FILE_WRITE_COMMAND` constants
- `src/gateway/node-command-policy.ts` — `file.read` added to `SYSTEM_COMMANDS` (linux/macos/windows default-allowed); `file.write` added to `DEFAULT_DANGEROUS_NODE_COMMANDS` (requires explicit `allowCommands` opt-in)
- `src/agents/tools/nodes-tool.ts` — `file_pull`/`file_push` actions, schema fields, 64 MB hard size cap
- `src/agents/tools/nodes-tool.test.ts` — 4 new tests for `file_pull`/`file_push`

## RPC contract

Node-side implementation (iOS/Android/Linux apps) is out of scope for this PR. The contract is:

**`file.read`** request:
```ts
{ path: string; encoding?: "base64" | "utf8" }
```
response payload:
```ts
{ path: string; encoding: "base64" | "utf8"; data: string; size: number; mimeType?: string }
```

**`file.write`** request:
```ts
{ path: string; data: string; encoding?: "base64" | "utf8"; mkdirs?: boolean; overwrite?: boolean }
```
response payload:
```ts
{ path: string; size: number; ok: true }
```

## Security

- `file.read`: non-dangerous, available on host nodes by default (linux/macos/windows)
- `file.write`: gated behind `DEFAULT_DANGEROUS_NODE_COMMANDS`, requires explicit `gateway.nodes.allowCommands: ["file.

items.title: feat: add Vertex AI provider support with native SDK integration
items.content:
- Add native anthropic-vertex provider using OpenClaw's Vertex SDK instead of proxy URL rewriting; maps GCP+non-Google credentials to {provider}-vertex provider key with real Vertex AI baseUrl
- Remap model entries (anthropic/* → anthropic-vertex/*) when only the vertex variant is configured
- Skip gateway route for Vertex SDK providers; traffic flows through MITM proxy with transparent GCP credential injection
- Configure claw deployment with stub ADC file and Vertex env vars (GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_PROJECT_ID)
- Support authorized_user GCP credentials alongside service accounts
- Infer domain and apiKey defaults for known providers (google, anthropic), making CR configuration more concise
- Add provider setup documentation (docs/provider-setup.md)
- Use timestamp-based image tags for reliable dev redeployments


Made-with: Cursor

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Release Notes

* **New Features**
  * Simplified credential configuration: `domain` field now optional for known providers (google, anthropic); system auto-detects values
  * Added Vertex AI support for additional LLM providers via GCP
  * Relaxed validation: `apiKey` required only when `type` is set to `apiKey` without a provider

* **Documentation**
  * Added comprehensive provider setup guide with provider-specific configuration examples

* **Improvements**
  * Enhanced credential type detection for GCP authentication
  * Improved deployment image tagging for development builds

items.content:
**📅 AI Agent & Skills 每日情报简报 (2026-04-16)**

items.author: github-actions[bot]

items.title: fix: respect sandbox mode off — prevent exec host auto-resolution to sandbox
items.content:
## Summary

Implements the fix from openclaw/openclaw#67468 to prevent exec host auto-resolution to sandbox when sandbox mode is explicitly disabled.

## Problem

When sandbox mode is set to 'off' (or no sandbox is configured), `exec.host: 'auto'` still resolved to sandbox if a sandbox config object existed, because the sandbox availability check used `Boolean(defaults?.sandbox)` which is truthy for any object, even `{mode: 'off'}`.

This broke security boundaries: if a user explicitly disabled sandboxing, they expected `exec.host: 'auto'` to fall back to the standard (non-sandboxed) execution path.

## Fix

**SECURITY FIX**: Changed sandbox availability check from:
```typescript
const sandboxAvailable = Boolean(defaults?.sandbox);
```
to:
```typescript
const sandboxAvailable = Boolean(defaults?.sandbox?.mode && defaults.sandbox.mode !== 'off');
```

The fix checks that mode exists AND is not 'off' before treating sandbox as available.

## Changes

1. **packages/schemas/src/index.ts**: Added `SandboxConfig`, `SandboxMode`, `ExecHost`, `ExecToolsConfig`, and `AgentToolPolicy` schemas
2. **src/openclaw/role-agent-adapter.ts**:
   - Added `isSandboxAvailable()` function that implements the security fix
   - Added `resolveExecHost()` function to resolve 'auto' to 'sandbox' or 'gateway'
   - Updated `OpenClawAgentConfig` to include `sandboxConfig` and `execHost` fields
   - Updated `toAgentConfig()` and `getAllAgentConfigs()` to accept sandbox config

## Edge Cases

- Empty sandbox config `{}`: mode undefined → sandbox unavailable (safe default)
- Partial config `{docker: {image: 'x'}}`: mode undefined → unavailable
- Explicit mode: `'off'` → unavailable
- Explicit mode: `'non-main'` or `'all'` → available
- Explicit host: `'sandbox'` still works regardless of mode

## Testing

Regression test verifying:
- `auto` → `gateway` when mode is `'off'`
- `auto` → `sandbox` when mode is `'all'` or `'non-main'`
- Explicit `host: 'sandbox'` still works regardless of mode

Fixes Un

items.title: Bug: Model selector not working in Chat panel (only Primary Model setting works)
items.content:
# Bug: Model selector not working in Chat panel (only Primary Model setting works)

## Problem

The model selector dropdown in the **Chat panel** does not actually change the response model. Only the **Primary Model** setting in the Agent section works as expected; changing models in the Chat panel has no effect.

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open OpenClaw’s Control UI.
2. In the **Chat** panel, switch the model using the model selector dropdown.
3. Submit a message or use any tool.
4. The response model remains the same as whatever is set as the Agent’s Primary Model. The chat dropdown selection is ignored.

---

## Expected Behavior
- Switching models in the Chat panel dropdown should change the model used for that reply or turn.

## Actual Behavior
- Model selector in Chat does nothing. The model used always matches the Agent’s Primary Model.

---

## Notes
- Checked as of 2026-04-16: no existing issue found for this bug.
- Many users may assume the selector works; the UI is misleading as-is.
- **Severity:** Moderate (UI inconsistency, blocks per-turn model switching as expected).

---

items.title: fix: prevent socket leaks in streaming HTTP responses via FinalizationRegistry
items.content:
## Summary

When streaming HTTP responses are abandoned by consumers (generator abandoned before completion), undici sockets could leak because `reader.cancel()` and `reader.releaseLock()` were only called on natural stream end or explicit generator return - not on stream abandonment.

This PR adds a `FinalizationRegistry` safety net that calls `reader.cancel()` followed by `reader.releaseLock()` when the stream reader is garbage collected before the generator completes.

## Changes

- `packages/runtime/src/inference/connectors.ts`: Added `FinalizationRegistry` to `collectChatStream` function
- `src/llm/streaming.ts`: Added `FinalizationRegistry` to both `openaiStream` and `minimaxStream` functions

## How it works

1. When a streaming response reader is created, it's registered with a module-level `FinalizationRegistry`
2. The registry holds a weak reference to the reader
3. If the generator is abandoned and the reader is GC'd before natural completion:
   - The registry callback fires
   - `reader.cancel()` is called to release the underlying socket back to the pool
   - `reader.releaseLock()` releases the reader lock
4. In the normal completion path (`finally` block), we:
   - Unregister from the registry (avoid double cleanup)
   - Call `reader.cancel()` (safe to call even if stream is done)
   - Call `reader.releaseLock()`

## Fixes

- Fixes UnlikeOtherAI/Nessie#66
- Source: openclaw/openclaw#67461

items.title: browser: restore strict default SSRF hostname guard
items.content:
### Motivation
- A recent change relaxed browser SSRF protections for the default `ssrfPolicy = {}` causing hostname-based browser navigation to bypass redirect-hop inspection and hostname/IP-literal enforcement, enabling DNS-rebinding SSRF vectors.
- The intent of this PR is to restore the original fail-closed behavior so unconfigured installs still get strict browser-side SSRF guards unless private-network access is explicitly allowed.

### Description
- Restore redirect-hop inspection requirement by changing `requiresInspectableBrowserNavigationRedirects` to return `Boolean(ssrfPolicy && !isPrivateNetworkAllowedByPolicy(ssrfPolicy))` so any present policy that disallows private networks triggers inspection (`extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.ts`).
- Re-enable strict hostname/IP-literal enforcement for browser navigations when a policy is present and private-network access is not allowed by removing the narrow `dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork === false` gate in `assertBrowserNavigationAllowed` (`extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.ts`).
- Update tests to reflect strict-default behavior: the navigation guard tests now assert that the default `{}` policy blocks hostname navigation and that redirect-hop inspection is required when private-network mode is disabled (`extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.test.ts`).
- Adjust the attach-only tabs route test expectation to match strict-mode URL redaction behavior (empty `url` when strict policy redacts hostname) (`extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/tabs.attach-only.test.ts`).

### Testing
- Ran `pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.test.ts` and the file passed (`25 tests` all green).
- Ran `pnpm test` for the three touched browser extension tests: `extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.test.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/tabs.attach-only.test.ts`, and `extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.remote-profile-tab-ops.fallback.test.ts`, an

items.title: Validate and harden user-controlled bracket-notation injection findings
items.content:
## Summary

Sourcery reported a high-severity injection finding for user-controlled bracket notation on objects in Express routes, including `server/src/routes/agents.ts` and another location.

## Goal

Validate whether request-controlled keys can reach object/property access in a way that changes behavior or bypasses invariants, compare with upstream, and patch any real injection surface.

## Initial scope

- inspect all reported bracket-notation sites
- classify key sources and reachable effects
- patch unsafe cases and add targeted regression coverage

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报 (2026-04-16)

## 1. 今日速览

OpenClaw 项目今日保持极高的活跃度，过去24小时内共有 500 条 Issue 更新与 500 条 PR 更新。项目发布了 **v2026.4.15-beta.1** 版本，重点增强了控制面板的模型认证状态监控能力。然而，社区面临严峻挑战，**Onboarding/Configure 流程出现大规模崩溃**（`TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')`），导致大量新用户无法完成初始化配置，该问题成为今日关注焦点。此外，关于 Agent 身份验证与信任机制的 RFC 讨论热度不减，显示出社区对 AI Agent 安全架构的深度探索。

## 2. 版本发布

**v2026.4.15-beta.1** 已发布。
- **更新亮点**：Control UI 新增 **Model Auth status card**，支持一键查看 OAuth Token 健康状态及 Provider 速率限制压力，并在 Token 即将过期或已过期时发出提醒。后端新增 `models.authStatus` 网关方法（剥离敏感信息并缓存 60s）。
- **影响**：提升了多模型环境下的运维可观测性，减少了因 Token 过期或限流导致的静默失败。

## 3. 项目进展

今日共有 149 个 PR 合并/关闭，项目在稳定性修复与生态集成方面取得明显进展：

- **[CLOSED] #43961 fix(gateway): SIGKILL stale process**  
  修复了 Gateway 重启/停止时，若优雅关闭超时可能导致的僵尸进程问题，确保新进程启动前旧进程彻底清理。
- **[OPEN] #65554 feat: WebChat Media Support v2**  
  为 WebChat 补全了图片、音频等多媒体支持，显著提升了 Web 端交互体验。
- **[OPEN] #35071 feat: Add Eval Framework for skill-creator**  
  推进 Skill 评估框架的开发，将支持技能测试、基准测试与触发分析，为 Skill 市场质量保驾护航。
- **[OPEN] #67475 fix(memory-lancedb): reject inbound envelope metadata**  
  修复了记忆模块将传输元数据误存为长期记忆的 Bug，防止 Prompt 污染。
- **[OPEN] #6748

### Bug type

Crash (process/app exits or hangs)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

A TypeError occurs during the initialization wizard when reaching the "Select channel (QuickStart)" step. The interface displays several undefined: undefined entries in the channel status list before crashing with Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim').

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the installation/initialization script: curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash (or openclaw init).
2. Configure a model provider (in my case, MiniMax OAuth).
3. Proceed through the wizard until the "Select channel" step.
4. The crash occurs automatically when trying to render the channel selection menu.

Program crash

2024.4.14

Ubuntu 24.04

npm global

MINIMAX

items.author: flaky-claw

items.title: feat: loop orchestrator — main process that drives the loop continuously
items.content:
## Summary

Implements the v0.1 loop orchestrator per issue #7 — the engine that ties every previously-built component (roadmap parser, state inspector, implementation agent, PR monitor, git operations) into one continuously running process. `runCycle` runs the state machine for a single tick; `startLoop` polls it forever, honouring file-based pause / stop signals.

Closes #7

## What landed

All new code lives under `src/core/loop/` (one file per concern, mirroring the `core/checks/` and `core/agents/` discipline) plus thin CLI adapters:

- **`src/core/loop/orchestrator.ts`** — `runCycle` + the `CycleResult` discriminated union (`action-taken | waiting | halted | milestone-complete`). The cycle is: parse roadmap → inspect repo → if open `claw/` PR exists, dispatch on `getPRVerdict` (approved → squash-merge + delete-branch + clear session; changes-requested → fetch blocking comments → `runFixCycle`; pending → wait; ci-failing → halt). If no open PR, spawn the implementation agent on the lowest-numbered open issue.
- **`src/core/loop/start-loop.ts`** — `startLoop` plus `LoopExitResult` and `formatCycle`. Polls `stop` → exit; polls `pause` → wait + retry; runs a cycle; logs the outcome; sleeps `pollInterval` seconds; repeats. Idle-warning emits a log line after 30 min with no `action-taken`. Logging failures NEVER propagate — disk health cannot break the loop.
- **`src/core/loop/control.ts`** — file-based pause/stop coordination via `.claw/control/{pause,stop}` so `claw pause` / `claw resume` / `claw stop` can signal a separate `claw start` process without IPC infrastructure. Loop polls between cycles, so signals are non-destructive: the in-flight action always completes first.
- **`src/core/loop/log.ts`** — append-only `.claw/loop.log` writer + tail reader, format `ISO-timestamp\tline\n` for grep / `tail -f` friendliness.
- **`src/core/loop/review-comments.ts`** — `fetchBlockingReviewComments` extracts the input the fix cycle needs: filters to `REVIEW_AGENT_HEADERS`

items.title: docs: accept Soma Heart Gate 6 stabilisation plan
items.content:
Moves docs/proposals/soma-heart-certificate-gate6-stabilisation.md from `Status: proposed` to `Status: accepted`, clearing Gate 6 of the ADR-0005 gate sequence for Soma Heart certificates. This PR is docs-only. It does not merge implementation, edit package manifests or exports, touch source files, modify the vector corpus, change the spec, change ADR-0005 or ADR-0004, or change credential-rotation semantics. Gate 7 remains blocked and downstream to claw-net.

## What this acceptance pins

- **Package**: `soma-heart` (source of truth; no new top-level package).
- **Subpath export**: `soma-heart/certificate` (matches the existing `soma-heart/credential-rotation` subpath pattern).
- **Conceptual source directory**: `packages/soma-heart/src/certificate/` (conceptual ownership only; no file is created by this acceptance).
- **`soma-sense` re-export posture**: observer-safe read path only -- canonicalisation helpers, certificate identifier derivation, signature-input construction, vector-corpus loading and conformance helpers, profile validation, claim-vocabulary validation, evidence-vocabulary validation, and the failure-mode / error mapping (areas 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 12). Signing, evidence binding, rail adapter wiring, rotation lookup, and verifier-policy evaluation are NOT re-exported to observer-only installs.
- **Public / internal API boundary**: areas 1-7, 10-binding, 11-interface, and 12 public under `soma-heart/certificate`; area 8 verifier-policy evaluator public but full-install only; area 9 rotation lookup adapter and area 11 concrete adapter wiring internal-only. Deferred profiles, claims, evidence kinds, and `fulfillment-receipt-bound` are not shipped by Gate 6.
- **Vector conformance**: any future implementation PR, if separately authorised, MUST produce canonical bytes, certificate identifiers, and signature-input hashes that exactly match every entry in `test-vectors/soma-heart-certificate/v0.1/manifest.json`. Mismatches are `canonicalisation-diverge

items.title: blog: I Built an Always-On AI Assistant in a Day
items.content:
## New Blog Post

**Slug:** `building-always-on-ai-assistant`

~1,850 word technical post about moving the personal AI assistant from a home System76 Meerkat to AWS EC2 with OpenTofu, Tailscale, and Discord.

### Covers
- The cattle-for-compute, pet-for-memory architecture (EC2 + EBS)
- Cloud-init debugging saga (Ubuntu 24.04 gotchas, TTY-less installers)
- Webhook bridge pattern (Claude on Mac → Tailscale → Hermes → Discord)
- Threat model conversation leading to approvals.mode: off
- Why selective porting beat green-field

### Assets
- Architecture diagram on Bunny CDN: hermes-architecture.webp
- Data flow diagram on Bunny CDN: hermes-dataflow.webp

### Checklist
- [x] metadata.json complete with CDN hero image
- [x] fix-affiliate-metadata.py passed
- [x] validate-affiliate-articles.sh passed
- [x] pnpm next build passed (8GB instance)
- [x] No banned patterns (checked against CLAUDE.md)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Content-only changes to a blog post and its metadata; no runtime/business logic is modified beyond MDX imports/components.
> 
> **Overview**
> Updates the `building-always-on-ai-assistant` article from an OpenClaw-on-Meerkat build guide to a Hermes Agent on AWS/EC2 migration story, including new sections on IaC (OpenTofu), persistent EBS state, cloud-init troubleshooting, Tailscale hardening, and a webhook bridge workflow.
> 
> Refreshes blog metadata (title/date/description/image/tags) and updates the MDX to use `GitHubRepoCard` and new hero/diagram images, while removing the prior long-form integration/hardware walkthrough content.

I was planning to finalize a blog on a polycrisis ''Minsky moment'': the polycrisis version has an additional and more dangerous feature: war, energy, food, el Nino, climate, politics, cost of living, cross-domain contagion. items.title: feat: git operations — branch lifecycle, rebase, squash merge, conflict handling
items.content:
## Summary

Implements the v0.1 git-operations layer per issue #6. All branch creation, rebasing, merging, and cleanup now flow through `src/core/git/operations.ts` — the single entry point CLAUDE.md designates for "all git operations". Nothing else in the codebase calls the raw Octokit git APIs.

Closes #6

## What landed

A single module (`src/core/git/operations.ts`) + its test file. All new code, no existing files touched:

- **`createBranch(client, repo, branch)`** — reads the default branch's name and tip SHA via `repos.get` + `git.getRef`, then creates `refs/heads/{branch}` pointing at the tip via `git.createRef`.
- **`rebaseOnDefault(client, repo, branch)`** — finds the open PR for the branch (paginates `pulls.list` with `head=owner:branch`), then invokes `pulls.updateBranch`. A 422-with-"conflict" response surfaces as `GitConflictError`.
- **`mergeDefaultIntoBranch(client, repo, branch)`** — reads the default branch name, then calls `repos.merge` with `base: branch, head: defaultBranch`. A 409 response surfaces as `GitConflictError`. The 204 "already up to date" response resolves successfully.
- **`squashMerge(client, repo, prNumber, issueTitle, issueNumber)`** — calls `pulls.merge` with `merge_method: "squash"`, the exact commit title `feat: {title} (closes #{N})` (so GitHub auto-closes the issue), and `Closes #{N}` in the body (mirroring the implementation agent's PR body for audit-trail consistency).
- **`deleteBranch(client, repo, branch)`** — calls `git.deleteRef`. Safety-gated on the `claw/` prefix so the loop can never delete a human branch.
- **`branchName`** — re-exported from `src/core/agents/branch-name.js` so the git module is the canonical entry point without duplicating logic (issue #3's slug tests remain the single source of truth for branch-name semantics).
- **`GitConflictError`** + **`isGitConflictError`** — typed error subclass of `ClawError` so the orchestrator (issue #7) can route a conflict to the fix cycle rather than halting the loop

items.title: [Bug]: Talk Mode audio silently drops on macOS when agent turn includes tool calls
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

When an agent reply follows one or more tool calls in the same turn, TTS audio is generated on the server but never played on the macOS app. The response is visible in server CLI and chat history, but the Mac app produces no audio. Replies with zero tool calls always play correctly.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Enable Talk Mode on macOS app (remote mode)
2. Ask any question that triggers a tool call (exec, file read, API call)
3. The spoken reply will be silent
4. Ask a pure conversational question with no tool calls → audio plays fine

### Expected behavior

TTS audio should play regardless of whether tool calls occurred in the same turn.

### Actual behavior

The macOS app plays no audio.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.14

### Operating system

macOS 26.3 (for the macOS app), Ubuntu (for the Open Claw server)

### Install method

dmg (for the macOS app), npm (for the Open Claw server)

### Model

anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6

### Provider / routing chain

not sure, let me know if you need more info here

### Additional provider/model setup details

Even a near-instant tool call (echo "hi", ~0ms) causes the drop. Duration is irrelevant — the mere presence of any tool call in the turn is sufficient to trigger the bug.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

Not severe, but a major impediment to using macOS's Talk Mode

### Additional information

No known workarounds. User must read responses visually when tool calls are involved.

items.title: Chat auto-greet UX + Telegram bot info CTA
items.content:
## Summary

A bundle of post-setup polish based on real device walkthroughs.

### ChatPopup — auto-greet bootstrap UX
- Split bootstrap state from real generation: new `isBootstrappingHistory` flag covers the WS history round-trip + the gap before the auto-greet starts streaming. `sendMessage`'s re-entry guard, the stop button, and `onThinkingChange` remain keyed only on `sending` (real runs); the typing bubble + greeting placeholder + empty-state suppression key on `isBootstrappingHistory` too so the user sees feedback the whole time.
- New `chat.greetingPlaceholder` string ("Waiting for the Claw to wake up…") in all 10 locales — input is locked while bootstrap or auto-greet is pending so the user can't race the agent.

### Settings → Telegram — surface bot info
- `/setup-api/telegram/status` now hits `api.telegram.org/getMe` and returns `{ username, firstName, link }` alongside `configured`.
  - 60s success cache, 5s failure cache (transient errors don't poison the long TTL).
  - Concurrent callers for the same token coalesce onto a single in-flight Promise via `inFlightFetch` Map (no thundering herd).
- `SettingsApp` shows the bot's display name, `@username`, and a Telegram-blue **"Open @<bot> in Telegram"** CTA. New `settings.openInTelegram` string with `{name}` interpolation across all 10 locales.

### CredentialsStep — safer post-rename redirect
- Build the new-hostname redirect URL via the `URL` API instead of string concatenation.
- Validate `newSetupUrl.origin` (protocol + port match `window.location`, hostname matches the regex-validated `<name>.local`) before any `window.location.replace`; falls back to `onNext()` if the check fails.
- On `TypeError("Failed to fetch")` during save, HEAD-probe the new URL (5s timeout) before redirecting; surface a visible error if the new hostname isn't reachable yet so the user isn't stranded on a dead URL.
- Spinner inside the Connect button while saving.

items.author: flaky-claw

items.title: Feature: openclaw gateway preflight — validate config + engines without binding port
items.content:
## Problem

The gateway has no pre-start validation. A broken config or incompatible engine is only discovered **after** the port is bound and requests are in flight — at which point sessions are already corrupted and the only signal is a generic error surfaced to end users.

This is not theoretical. Two concrete failures in the last month:

### v4.14 regression — 2 hours of silent failure

The `lossless-claw` context engine shipped with a mismatched registry ID. On instantiation, every execution lane (main, nested, cron, slug-generator) hit the same error:

```
registry-nEl7QZ30.js:237: info.id must match registered id 'lossless-claw'
```

The gateway started, bound the port, accepted connections — and failed every single request with a generic "something went wrong" in Telegram. No structured error, no startup abort. Root cause required grepping raw gateway logs. Since 4.13 was never published (4.12 → 4.14-beta.1 → 4.14), rollback meant going back to 4.12 manually.

A preflight check running the same instantiation code path would have caught this before the gateway ever accepted a connection.

### #66401 — `openclaw update` races against live sessions

`npm install -g openclaw` runs without stopping the LaunchAgent. The install can overwrite files while the gateway is serving requests, corrupting config and session state mid-flight. There is no atomic update path.

## Proposal: `openclaw gateway preflight`

A first-class subcommand that exercises the full startup code path — without binding the port or touching session state.

### What it does

| Step | Validates | Catches |
|------|-----------|---------|
| 1. Parse config JSON | Well-formed JSON, required fields present | Syntax errors, missing keys, truncated files from interrupted writes |
| 2. Validate config against schema | Types, allowed values, plugin references | `gateway.bind: "all"` and similar invalid enum values |
| 3. Instantiate context engine registry | Engine ID matching, dependency resolution | T

items.title: feat: PR monitor — wait for review verdicts, read results, decide next action
items.content:
## Summary

Implements the v0.1 PR monitor per issue #4. After the implementation agent opens a PR, the orchestrator needs to know what to do with it next — wait, merge, fix, or halt. `getPRVerdict` collapses the full pipeline state (CI conclusion, review-agent comment headers, Review Summary verdict, and `mergeable_state`) into one of four `PRVerdict` values the orchestrator will switch on in issue #7.

Closes #4

## What landed

All new code is under `src/core/agents/` (one file per concern, mirroring `core/checks/` and the other agent modules):

- **`src/core/agents/pr-monitor.ts`** — `getPRVerdict(client, repo, prNumber)` plus three pure helpers (`hasFailingRun`, `allReviewsPosted`, `latestReviewSummary`) and the `PRVerdict` / `PRMetadata` / `CIRun` / `PRMonitorDeps` types. Every external boundary is injectable.
- **`tests/core/agents/pr-monitor.test.ts`** — 34 cases spanning the decision table, the three helpers, and the wrapper's error behaviour.

## Decision table

Ordering mirrors the spec exactly — `ci-failing` wins over approvals, latest summary wins over stale summaries, and nothing merges until `mergeable_state === "clean"`.

| Condition (first match wins) | Verdict |
|---|---|
| Any CI run has a failing conclusion | `ci-failing` |
| Fewer than five `REVIEW_AGENT_HEADERS` present in comments | `pending` |
| No `## Review Summary` comment posted yet | `pending` |
| Latest summary contains `### Blocking Issues` | `changes-requested` |
| Latest summary doesn't contain `### Ready to merge` | `pending` |
| `mergeable_state !== "clean"` | `pending` |
| Otherwise | `approved` |

The "latest summary wins" rule matches the `Fail if changes requested` step in `src/core/templates/ci.yml` — after a fix cycle the summary job reposts, and both the monitor and branch protection read the most recent one so they stay in agreement.

## Architecture alignment

- **Pure read operation.** No writes, no merges, no comments. The orchestrator (issue #7) turns the verdict into a

items.author: bbrysonelite-max

items.title: fix portable memory ownership across redeploys
items.content:
## Summary
Portable memory still does not meet the out-of-the-box ownership contract for managed pods.

The v0.8.13 hotfix stops `claw up` from aborting when host-side permission normalization hits `EPERM`, but that only masks the ownership mismatch. Managed services intentionally run under varying container UIDs, so a previous generation can leave `.claw-memory/<service>/memory/**` owned by a container UID that the host user cannot later `chmod`. On the next `claw up`, Clawdapus may continue with a warning while leaving files or directories effectively unwritable for the next runtime.

## Why this matters
Clawdapus defaults should not require operators to manually repair ownership after routine `claw up` / `claw down` cycles. If pods are managed out of the box, the portable memory surface must remain writable across redeploys without manual intervention.

## Constraints
- Managed services already support non-root runtime users; we cannot assume containers run as the invoking host UID.
- Portable memory is durable state under `.claw-memory/` and is pre-migrated before compose apply.
- Current host-side normalization cannot reliably fix ownership once a different UID owns the tree.
- Any fix should preserve cross-driver portability and not regress the existing non-root driver behavior.

## Proposed scope
1. Add a real normalization path that can repair modes/ownership for the portable memory tree even when the host user cannot `chmod` it directly.
2. Add stable per-service runtime ownership defaults so managed services stop drifting to arbitrary writer identities across redeploys.
3. Add regression coverage for the real failure mode: one generation writes memory under one UID with restrictive permissions, a later `claw up` under a different effective writer still succeeds without manual repair.

## Open questions
- Should normalization happen via a short-lived Docker helper container operating on the bind mount, via `docker compose run/exec`, or via a stricter fixed
items.author: mostlydev

items.title: feat: implementation agent — spawn, fix cycle, escalation
items.content:
## Summary

Implements the v0.1 implementation agent. Given an issue number, the orchestrator spawns `claude -p` with full project context on a `claw/issue-{N}-{slug}` branch, persists the session id to `.claw/sessions/{N}.json`, and opens a PR with `Closes #{N}` in the body. When reviewers request changes, the **same session** resumes via `claude -p --resume {sessionId}` — the agent that wrote the code is the agent that fixes it, so context never drifts between the implementation and fix paths.

Closes #3

## What landed

All new code is under `src/core/agents/` (one file per concern, mirroring the `core/setup/` + `core/checks/` layout):

- **`session.ts`** — `loadSession` / `saveSession` / `deleteSession` plus pure `serializeSession` / `parseSession` / `sessionPath` helpers. Re-exports `SessionFile` from `core/checks/types.ts` so CHECK 11's reader stays the single source of truth. The JSON validator rejects every shape CHECK 11 would reject.
- **`branch-name.ts`** — deterministic `branchName(issueNumber, title)` → `"claw/issue-{N}-{slug}"`. Slug is lowercased, non-alphanumerics collapse to single hyphens, capped at 40 chars with trailing-hyphen stripping after truncation, and falls back to `"issue"` for all-punctuation titles.
- **`prior-review-notes.ts`** — walks the GitHub timeline for `cross-referenced` events, filters to merged PRs via `pulls.get`, and harvests every comment body containing a standalone `#{N}` reference. The boundary regex `(?<![\w#])#N(?!\w)` is the load-bearing bit: it rejects longer numbers (`#35` doesn't match for `#3`), word-prefixed refs (`foo#3`), and markdown headings (`##3`).
- **`context.ts`** — pure string builders for the implementation prompt (issue title + body, target branch, README, ROADMAP, closed/open siblings, prior notes) and the fix prompt (every blocking review comment in full, the attempt number, and a strict "don't expand scope / don't refactor untouched code" instruction).
- **`claude.ts`** — `spawnImplementationSessio
items.author: pA1nD

items.title: Import tasks from other benchmarks (claw-eval, wild-claw-bench) via adapter layer
items.content:
## Proposal

Add a task-import adapter so ClawBench can run tasks defined by other browser-agent benchmarks — specifically `claw-eval` and `wild-claw-bench` to start, with a clear registration path for additional benchmarks — without forking their task repos.

## Why

- Every browser-agent benchmark defines tasks in slightly different schemas (WebArena-style JSON, Mind2Web step traces, VisualWebArena starting-state dumps, WorkArena goal-conditions, etc.). Today a research team that wants to reuse our submission-interception + five-layer recording has to hand-convert each task.
- Both `claw-eval` and `wild-claw-bench` target the same problem space we do (live-site agentic tasks). Running them through our harness gives a common scoring substrate — one judge pipeline, one recording format, one leaderboard row.
- This is cheap: our task schema (`task.json` with `url`, `instruction`, `time_limit`, rubric pointers) is a near-superset of what most benchmarks encode. The adapter is import-only; no outbound export needed yet.

## Current state

- Our tasks live under `test-cases/<category>/<name>/task.json`, loaded by `src/clawbench/run.py` via `paths.bundled_data_dir()`.
- `clawbench cases` lists bundled cases only. There is no mechanism to point at an external task dir or a third-party benchmark repo.
- No schema-conversion layer exists; `task.json` is parsed directly.

## Proposed design

### 1. Adapter layout

```
src/clawbench/adapters/
  __init__.py               # registry: {"clawbench-native": load_native, "claw-eval": load_claw_eval, ...}
  native.py                 # identity loader for our own task.json
  claw_eval.py              # claw-eval → ClawBenchTask
  wild_claw_bench.py        # wild-claw-bench → ClawBenchTask
  webarena.py               # web-arena-x/webarena + visualwebarena → ClawBenchTask
  mind2web.py               # OSU-NLP-Group/Mind2Web + Online-Mind2Web → ClawBenchTask
  webvoyager.py             # MinorJerry/WebVoyager → ClawBenchTask
  workaren

items.title: Fix/telegram config read bug
items.content:
## Summary

• Problem: Intermittent TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 't') during config read when Telegram is enabled
• Why it matters: CLI commands like openclaw status --deep intermittently crash when reading config with Telegram channel configured
• What changed: Added defensive error handling in loadBundledEntryModuleSync to properly handle loader failures and prevent caching of null/undefined modules
• What did NOT change: No changes to plugin loading architecture or caching semantics

## Change Type

• [x] Bug fix
• [ ] Feature
• [ ] Refactor required for the fix
• [ ] Docs
• [ ] Security hardening
• [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

• [x] Gateway / orchestration
• [ ] Skills / tool execution
• [ ] Auth / tokens
• [ ] Memory / storage
• [ ] Integrations
• [ ] API / contracts
• [ ] UI / DX
• [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

• Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/62844
• [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

• Root cause: When bundled channel entry module loading fails (e.g., via native require or jiti), the error handling could leave the module cache with an undefined/null value. Subsequent accesses would then fail with "Cannot read properties of undefined"
• Missing detection / guardrail: No guardrails checking if loaded module is valid before caching
• Contributing context: Intermittent failures suggest race conditions or timing issues in module loading where sometimes modules load correctly and sometimes they don't

## Regression Test Plan

  • [ ] Unit test

items.title: Add OpenAI-Codex provider: websocket client, protocol, translation, and routing
items.content:
### Motivation

- Introduce a dedicated `openai-codex` provider to integrate with a Codex app-server/broker over WebSocket without changing the existing OpenAI-compatible provider.
- Keep provider routing and model namespace separation (`openai-codex/...`) to avoid mixing legacy Anthropic OAuth paths and `openai_compat` logic.
- Provide a minimal translation layer from Claw `MessageRequest` to the Codex `UserInput` format and a protocol scaffold for JSON-RPC/WebSocket streaming.

### Description

- Added a new provider implementation `OpenAiCodexClient` in `rust/crates/api/src/providers/openai_codex.rs` that connects to a Codex app-server over WebSocket, performs `initialize` / `thread/start` / `turn/start` and exposes `send_message` and streaming via a new `MessageStream` type.
- Implemented the Codex JSON-RPC types in `openai_codex_protocol.rs` and a request translator `openai_codex_translate.rs` that converts `MessageRequest`/`InputMessage` into Codex `UserInput` items.
- Extended provider routing and metadata: added `ProviderKind::OpenAiCodex`, updated `metadata_for_model` to recognize `openai-codex/` model prefixes, and added wiring in `client.rs` to construct and dispatch `ProviderClient::OpenAiCodex` and stream variant handling.
- Exported new types from `lib.rs`, added tests under `rust/crates/api/tests` for protocol parsing and translation, and updated `Cargo.toml` to include `futures-util` and `tokio-tungstenite` dependencies.

### Testing

- Ran unit tests in the `api` crate including `parses_agent_message_delta_notification` which verifies `AgentMessageDeltaNotification` deserialization and it passed.
- Ran unit test `translates_simple_user_text_to_codex_user_input` which verifies `request_to_user_inputs` translation and it passed.
- Performed a crate build/test run for the modified `api` crate and the tests compiled and succeeded.

------

items.title: docs(claude-md): consolidate external-repo sign-off to human-friend phrasing
items.content:
## Summary

- Resolves conflicting sign-off rules in `claude-md/global.md`: the OLD "Keeping my human friend @idvorkin in the loop!" bullet (under GitHub Workflow) coexisted with a NEWER Claws-signature rule (added in 63a4ebc under Important Rules).
- Consolidates to ONE rule under "Important Rules", preserving the newer rule's structure (skip-on-own-repos guidance + example block) but swapping the signature text back to the human-friend phrasing.
- Rewrites the prose to match the simpler "tag Igor so he gets notified" rationale — drops the Claws-specific "makes AI-agent origin explicit / deep-links to igors-claws" justifications that no longer apply.
- Net: one rule, one signature, no Claws references remain in `claude-md/global.md`.

## Test plan

- [x] `grep -i claws claude-md/global.md` returns no hits
- [x] Only one "human friend" bullet remains (plus its example reuse)
- [x] Rule still positioned under Important Rules with skip-on-own-repos guidance and example block intact

items.title: ✨ [全端] Bot 资料页支持创建者修改 Bot 头像（后端 API 已就绪，缺客户端入口）
items.content:
## 反馈人

沟主

## 场景

Bot 创建者想给自己的龙虾换头像，但 Web / iOS / Android 三端的 Bot 资料页都没有修改头像的入口。

## 问题/诉求

> 给咱 bots 们换头像，哪个端都没有入口呀。

## 当前状态

- ✅ 后端 API 已就绪（dmworkim PR #806 已合并，支持 Bot 创建者修改头像）
- ❌ Web 端无入口
- ❌ iOS 端无入口
- ❌ Android 端无入口

## 期望效果

Bot 创建者在任意端打开自己 Bot 的资料页，点击头像可以修改（选择图片 → 裁剪 → 上传）。

## 初步方案

各端 Bot 资料页判断当前用户是否为该 Bot 的创建者，是则在头像上显示可编辑标识，点击触发修改流程，调用已有的后端 API。

## 关联

- pool#92（已关闭，当时只提了 iOS，且客户端未实现）
- dmworkim PR #806（后端 API）

## 原始来源

DMWork group:Bug反馈群 user:沟主 (uid:ba5c8d3f331a4907b8169e26a963f4ca)

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报 (2026-04-16)

## 1. 今日速览
OpenClaw 项目今日处于**极高活跃度**状态，过去 24 小时内共处理了 500 条 Issues 和 500 条 PRs，显示出极其旺盛的开发节奏和社区关注度。项目发布了 `v2026.4.15-beta.1` 版本，重点强化了模型认证状态的可视化监控能力。今日社区讨论焦点集中在 `v2026.4.14` 版本引入的严重回归问题，特别是安装向导崩溃、UI 闪烁及 OpenRouter/OpenAI 认证故障，大量用户报告了严重影响可用性的 Bug。尽管负面反馈较多，开发团队响应迅速，已有针对核心崩溃问题的修复 PR 正在合并中。

## 2. 版本发布
**发布版本：v2026.4.15-beta.1**
- **下载链接**: [Releases Tag](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.15-beta.1)
- **主要更新**:
  - **Control UI/Overview**: 新增“模型认证状态卡片”。该卡片可直观展示 OAuth 令牌的健康状况及提供商的速率限制压力。
  - **网关方法**: 引入新的 `models.authStatus` 网关方法，支持在剥离凭证信息的前提下缓存状态 60 秒。
  - **告警优化**: 当 OAuth 令牌即将过期或已过期时，UI 将呈现显著的注意力提示。
- **影响评估**: 这是一个维护性更新，旨在提升长期运行的稳定性与可观测性，未涉及破坏性变更。

## 3. 项目进展
今日核心进展主要集中在**修复昨日发布的 v2026.4.14 版本中的关键缺陷**。
- **核心崩溃修复**: PR [#67144](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67144) (CI/Release Checks) 和 PR [#67130](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/67130) (相关 Issue) 针对安装向导中普遍存在的 `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')` 进行了修复。该问题导致大量用户无法完成 "Select channel" 步骤，无论是否选择跳过都会崩溃。
- **进程管理优化**: PR [#43961](https://github​.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/4396

items.title: 🐛 [Adapter] 龙虾收到图片消息时图片未下载完就开始处理，导致第一次回复「看不到」
items.content:
## 端

适配器层（openclaw-channel-dmwork），影响所有端发送的图片

## 复现步骤

1. 在群里发一张图片
2. @龙虾 让它看图
3. 龙虾第一次回复说「还没看到」「没下载好」
4. 再@一次龙虾，这次才能看到图片

## 期望行为

龙虾收到图片消息后，应该先等图片下载完成、确认可访问，再交给 Agent 处理和回复。不应该在图片还没就绪时就开始处理。

## 实际行为

龙虾收到图片消息后立即开始处理，此时图片可能还没下载完或 URL 还未就绪，导致第一次处理时看不到图片内容，用户需要再@一次。

## 影响

- 用户体验差：需要重复操作
- 浪费 token：每次「看不到」的回复都消耗 token
- 高频场景：群里发图片给龙虾是常见操作

## 初步方案

适配器收到包含图片的消息时：
1. 先下载/验证图片 URL 可访问
2. 确认图片就绪后再将消息传递给 Agent
3. 如果下载超时，附带明确的错误信息告知 Agent「图片下载失败」，而不是让 Agent 自己猜

## 原始来源

DMWork group:Bug反馈群 user:吴明辉（辉哥） (uid:eca0702f83e048c7b6151b21b1a3b9de)

## 新文章

2. ## Bug 描述

## 复现步骤

1. 启动有道龙虾

2. 再安装 智企帝王蟹

3. 智企帝王蟹会提示gateway鉴权失败

## 期望行为
有道龙虾和 智企帝王蟹 都正常运行

有道龙虾正常运行，智企帝王蟹gateway异常，无响应。关闭有道龙虾，重装帝王蟹，就恢复正常

- OS：Tahoe
- 版本：26.4

- 必现

items.title: 初步部署使用了后提几个功能建议供参考
items.content:
考虑的场景主要是企业级智能体，提几点意见：

1. Agent创建是否可以参考龙虾和爱马仕，对智能体的人设、行为规范做一些约束，最重要的是支持编辑和修改。
2. 权限问题，企业不同部门和员工使用权限的问题，Skill/Agent归属问题。
3. 其它想到再补充

items.title: 热门免费二级域名汇总帖
items.content:
# 热门免费二级域名汇总帖（长期更新），目前入榜二十三家，供君选择。

> **本文整理自 Nodeloc 论坛**
> **原帖作者**：donitown (Tulku)
> **发布日期**：3月3日

汇总一下最近的免费二级域名，以个人喜好做了下梯队分类：

---

# 第一梯队：自然是支持托管CloudFlare的啦

1、**gv.uy** 它家还有hsk.sk、zz.cd（暂不支持CF）等多个域名， 这个在我心目中就是排第一，运维者是一个靠谱的“团队”，由一个人带领一群龙虾完成，非常高效高产。这家成长太迅速了，如雨后春笋节节高，每天都有新变化，如今各方大佬和资本纷至沓来，平台支持用户自己分发子域后，一下子上架了近20个新后缀，在这就不细数了，两个手都数不过来，各种超级短域。同时还拓展了每日签到领积分，领了积分换VPS的业务。
[注册地址](https://nic.i8.al/) [VPS8平台](https://vps8.zz.cd/)

2、**[eu.org](http://eu.org)** 这个不用说，声名远扬，现在不再审核了，据说jp.eu.org这个三级偶尔还有过审，大家可以碰碰运气。
[注册地址](https://nic.eu.org/)

3、**de5.net** 还有ccwu.cc、cc.cd、us.ci、bbroot等多个域名，且大多都支持托管CF
[注册地址](https://my.dnshe.com/)

**前三甲入榜理由：可托管CF，永久免费，一旦注册，终身无忧**

4、**hidns** 它家hidns.co和 hidns.vip 都能托管CF，这家也是极稳定的，要不是上车晚了，没有混到2099，否则绝对挤下de5入三甲。
[注册地址](https://www.hidoha.net/)

5、**f5.si** 来自日本，稳定，这是唯一一个我心心念，爱而不得的域名。缺点就是注册和托管CF门槛较高，需要日本手机号接码才能托管CF。这家也是一经注册，终身无忧的
[注册地址](https://ddns.kuku.lu/)

6、**pp.ua** 来自乌克兰，稳定，每隔三个月可以新注册免费域名，缺点是得绑卡
[注册地址](https://nic.ua/)

7、**nyc.mn** 曾经挺稳定的，后来换了主人
[注册地址](https://dot.nyc.mn/)

8、**us.kg** 这家是kg后缀二级域名之祖，一开始免费的，后来被薅烂，开始收费，同时还有多个后缀，比如xx.kg、[dpdns.org](http://dpdns.org)、qzz.io也都支持托管CF
[注册地址](https://domain.digitalplat.org/)

9、**indevs.in** 这家支持CF，最近新增了域名sryze.cc(暂不支持CF）
[注册地址](https://domain.stackryze.com/)

# 第一梯队入榜理由：可托管CF

---

# 第二梯队：使用年限长，不用担心忘记续费

10、**loc.cc** 这就是本论坛提供的域名啦，能用上一万年！一万年！其他后缀也很丰富，最近又加入了rr.kg
[注册地址](https://domain.nodeloc.com/)

11、**ae.kg** 这家在本论坛也很出名，拥有后缀也很多，aekg也已进入PSL名单，等待kevin开放加入CF托管，最近加入了cs.do, 使用年限也接近永久，期望kevin哪天节日给账号增加点域名额度
[注册地址](https://registry.kevsrv.me/)

12、**[9v4.net](http://9v4.net)** 这家的后缀也很多啦，有ug.cx、ww.fyi、hiv-info.com等，目前只有9v4.net可以工单申请永久，工单响应很快！
[注册地址](https://store.9v4.com/)

13、**fj.kg** 这家慷慨地开放了单字前缀申请，近日又新上架了ccp.tw、awa.bz、844880.xyz以及一个超长脑子秀逗域名。无本地NS，建议托管在VPS8平台。
[注册地址](https://nic.fj.kg/)

14、**nc.to** 这家有7个超短域名供选择，品相挺不错的，注册要github点星，具体后缀有i11.eu、int.yt、nc.to、rh.to、yn.to、gw.to，还有一个n8.to需要IFP Key，我也不知道IFP是啥，一个账号有3个免费域名的额度。支持托管三方NS，目前没看到有效期，默认永久免费吧
[注册地址](https://int.yt)

15、**furry.gd** 作者一口气上了六个后缀

items.title: fix(ports): map host DASHBOARD_PORT to sandbox port 18789 in forward command (#1925)
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes #1925.

`NEMOCLAW_DASHBOARD_PORT` lets users choose which **host** port the dashboard is reachable on, but the OpenClaw gateway inside the sandbox always listens on port **18789** — that port is baked into the sandbox image at build time via `CHAT_UI_URL=http://127.0.0.1:18789` in the `Dockerfile`.

Previously, `getDashboardForwardTarget()` and `ensureSandboxPortForward()` both passed `DASHBOARD_PORT` as the remote (sandbox-side) port, so setting `NEMOCLAW_DASHBOARD_PORT=19000` generated the wrong tunnel:

**Before fix** (`NEMOCLAW_DASHBOARD_PORT=19000`):
```
openshell forward start --background 19000 my-sandbox
```
→ maps `host:19000 → sandbox:19000`. The dashboard is on `sandbox:18789` (baked at image build time). Nothing answers on sandbox:19000 → `curl: (52) Empty reply from server`.

**After fix** (same env var):
```
openshell forward start --background 19000:18789 my-sandbox
```
→ maps `host:19000 → sandbox:18789`. The dashboard is reachable → HTML response.

**Default case (no override) — unchanged:**
```
openshell forward start --background 18789 my-sandbox
```

**WSL + custom port — also fixed:**
```
openshell forward start --background 0.0.0.0:19000:18789 my-sandbox
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/lib/ports.ts` | Add `SANDBOX_DASHBOARD_PORT = 18789` — the hardcoded sandbox-side port |
| `src/lib/onboard.ts` | `getDashboardForwardTarget()` appends `:SANDBOX_DASHBOARD_PORT` when host port differs |
| `src/nemoclaw.ts` | `ensureSandboxPortForward()` recovery path uses split-port logic for the non-agent case |
| `test/onboard.test.ts` | Update WSL test; add default-port and non-WSL custom-port cases |

## Test plan

- [x] `npx vitest run --project cli test/onboard.test.ts` — 116 passed (includes 3 new cases for default port, custom loopback, and custom WSL)
- [x] `npm run build:cli` — clean compile, no new errors
- [x] Pre-existing failures in `runtime-shell.test.ts` / `nim.test.ts` reproduce identically on `upstre

items.title: bootstrap: extract monolith into modular P0–P4 phase files
items.content:
## Summary

Restructures the 1200-line `CLIENT-BOOTSTRAP-PROMPT.md` into a modular, resumable system of 5 phase files plus an index router and two visual overview documents.

---

## Bootstrap file structure

```
architecture/plans/bootstrap/
├── INDEX.md                     ← Entry point — paste into Claude Code
├── CLIENT-BOOTSTRAP-PROMPT.md  ← Original monolith (retained as reference)
├── OVERVIEW.md                  ← Executive overview + Mermaid diagram
├── overview.html                ← Rich HTML visual illustration
├── p0-accounts.md               ← Manual account setup (Claude Chat guided)
├── p1-local-setup.md            ← Local machine + permissions + skills
├── p2-discovery.md              ← Business interview + website scaffold
├── p3-agents.md                 ← Full agent team build
└── p4-schedules.md              ← Schedules + verification
```

---

## What each phase file covers

### P0 — Accounts (`p0-accounts.md`)
Pure browser work, zero code. Claude Chat guides the user through:
- Claude Desktop Pro install
- GitHub, Vercel, Supabase account creation
- Supabase project + API key copy
- Email platform setup (Brevo recommended)
- Telegram bot creation (optional)
- Credentials summary checklist

**Key decision:** All manual account setup is front-loaded here so the rest of the bootstrap is fully automated.

---

### P1 — Local Setup (`p1-local-setup.md`)
Two stages, both automated by Claude:

**Stage A (Cowork — screen control):**
- Installs git, node, Vercel CLI, Supabase CLI
- Authenticates GitHub via `gh auth login`
- Pre-seeds all ~160 Claude Code permissions into `~/.claude/settings.local.json` via a Python3 merge script — **no permission prompts appear for the rest of the bootstrap**

**Stage B (Code):**
- Git identity config, project folder + `git init`
- Global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and local project `CLAUDE.md` (placeholder, updated in P2)
- Full folder structure (`agents/`, `content/`, `resources/`, `standup/`, `working_files/`, `website/`)
items.author: TracNg99

items.title: Respect sandbox mode: off — prevent exec host auto-resolution to sandbox when sandbox is explicitly disabled
items.content:
## Problem

When `sandbox.mode: 'off'` is configured (or when no sandbox is configured at all), `exec.host: 'auto'` still resolves to the sandbox target if a sandbox config object exists in the config tree. This is because the sandbox availability check used `Boolean(defaults?.sandbox)` which is truthy for ANY config object, even `{mode: 'off'}`.

This breaks security boundaries: if a user explicitly disables sandboxing, they expect `exec.host: 'auto'` to fall back to the standard (non-sandboxed) execution path.

## OpenClaw Source

- **PR:** openclaw/openclaw#67468 (fixes #58885)
- **Severity:** Medium — silent security boundary bypass when sandbox is explicitly disabled

## Root Cause

```typescript
// BEFORE (incorrect):
const sandboxAvailable = Boolean(defaults?.sandbox);

// AFTER (correct):
const sandboxAvailable = Boolean(defaults?.sandbox?.mode && defaults.sandbox.mode !== 'off');
```

The fix checks that `mode` exists AND is not `'off'` before treating sandbox as available.

## Nessie Fit

Nessie has a tool layer with bash/exec commands (`bash`, `file read/write`, `glob`, `grep`). If Nessie adds sandbox configuration, users who explicitly disable sandboxing expect the exec host to NOT route through sandbox. This fix ensures explicit user intent is honored.

## Implementation

1. Find the exec host resolution logic in `src/` (likely in the tool execution or agent runtime layer)
2. Change sandbox availability check from `Boolean(config?.sandbox)` to `Boolean(config?.sandbox?.mode && config.sandbox.mode !== 'off')`
3. Add a regression test: auto → gateway when mode is 'off', auto → sandbox when mode is 'all' or 'non-main'
4. Edge cases:
   - Empty sandbox config `{}`: mode is undefined → treated as unavailable (safe default)
   - Partial config `{docker: {image: 'x'}}`: mode undefined → unavailable
   - Explicit `host: 'sandbox'` still works regardless of mode

## Label

`enhancement`

items.title: feat: implement multi-user access and invite flows
items.content:
## Thinking Path

> - Paperclip is the control plane for autonomous AI companies.
> - V1 needs to stay local-first while also supporting shared, authenticated deployments.
> - Human operators need real identities, company membership, invite flows, profile surfaces, and company-scoped access controls.
> - Agents and operators also need the existing issue, inbox, workspace, approval, and plugin flows to keep working under those authenticated boundaries.
> - This branch accumulated the multi-user implementation, follow-up QA fixes, workspace/runtime refinements, invite UX improvements, and release-branch conflict resolution.
> - This pull request consolidates that branch onto the current `master` branch as a single reviewable PR.
> - The benefit is a complete multi-user implementation path with tests and docs carried forward without dropping existing branch work.

## What Changed

- Added authenticated human-user access surfaces: auth/session routes, company user directory, profile settings, company access/member management, join requests, and invite management.
- Added invite creation, invite landing, onboarding, logo/branding, invite grants, deduped join requests, and authenticated multi-user E2E coverage.
- Tightened company-scoped and instance-admin authorization across board, plugin, adapter, access, issue, and workspace routes.
- Improved issue chat, inbox, assignee identity rendering, sidebar/account/company navigation, workspace routing, and execution workspace reuse behavior for multi-user operation.
- Added and updated server/UI tests covering auth, invites, membership, issue workspace inheritance, plugin authz, inbox/chat behavior, and multi-user flows.
- Merged current `public-gh/master` into this branch, resolved all conflicts, and verified no `pnpm-lock.yaml` or database migration files are included in this PR diff.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec vitest run server/src/__tests__/issues-service.test.ts ui/src/components/IssueChatThread.test.tsx ui/src/pages

items.title: feat: 更新OpenClaw版本到v2026.4.14 - 包含重大不兼容变更，需要后续适配
items.content:
## 自动版本更新
### 版本变化
v2026.3.23 → v2026.4.14
### 更新等级
minor
### 重大变更
✅ 包含重大不兼容变更，需要完整适配
### 官方Release说明
OpenClaw `2026.4.14` is another broad quality release focused on model provider with explicit turn improvements for GPT-5 family and channel provider issues. Additionally we improved overal performance with refactors to our underlying core codebase.

## Changes

- OpenAI Codex/models: add forward-compat support for `gpt-5.4-pro`, including Codex pricing/limits and list/status visibility before the upstream catalog catches up. (#66453) Thanks @jepson-liu.
- Telegram/forum topics: surface human topic names in agent context, prompt metadata, and plugin hook metadata by learning names from Telegram forum service messages. (#65973) Thanks @ptahdunbar.

## Fixes

- Agents/Ollama: forward the configured embedded-run timeout into the global undici stream timeout tuning so slow local Ollama runs no longer inherit the default stream cutoff instead of the operator-set run timeout. (#63175) Thanks @mindcraftreader and @vincentkoc.
- Models/Codex: include `apiKey` in the codex provider catalog output so the Pi ModelRegistry validator no longer rejects the entry and silently drops all custom models from every provider in `models.json`. (#66180) Thanks @hoyyeva.
- Tools/image+pdf: normalize configured provider/model refs before media-tool registry lookup so image and PDF tool runs stop rejecting valid Ollama vision models as unknown just because the tool path skipped the usual model-ref normalization step. (#59943) Thanks @yqli2420 and @vincentkoc.
- Slack/interactions: apply the configured global `allowFrom` owner allowlist to channel block-action and modal interactive events, require an expected sender id for cross-verification, and reject ambiguous channel types so interactive triggers can no longer bypass the documented allowlist intent in channels without a `users` list. Open-by-default behavior is preserved when no allowlists are configured. (#66028) Thanks @eleq
items.author: Mr-tooth

items.title: docs: cross-project agent memory adoption guide
items.content:
## Summary
- Adds `docs/cross-project-memory.md` — a walkthrough for using schist MCP as a cross-project agent memory backend in any Claude project
- Establishes the `project:<slug>` tag convention for scoping memories without a schema change
- Zero code changes

## Why
The memory subsystem (tables in `~/.openclaw/memory/agent-state.db`, shared across all projects per user, scoped by `SCHIST_AGENT_ID`) has been fully built and tested (40 jest tests in `mcp-server/tests/memory*.test.ts`) but had no user-facing documentation.

Key insight: the DB path has no vault dependency — it is user-home-level by default. That makes the subsystem a cross-project memory layer **today, with no schist code changes**. What was missing is the adoption layer: how to wire it into a non-schist Claude project and what convention to use for project scoping.

## What the doc covers
- DB location, override via `SCHIST_MEMORY_DB`, relationship to vault DB
- Minimal `.mcp.json` fragment for wiring schist MCP into a non-schist project
- Tool surface: `search_memory` / `get_agent_state` / `list_domains` read-only by default; `add_memory` / `set_agent_state` / `delete_agent_state` gated behind `request_capabilities({capability: "write"})`
- `SCHIST_AGENT_ID` identity enforcement on writes (`CONFIG_ERROR` if unset, `VALIDATION_ERROR` on owner mismatch)
- `project:<slug>` tag convention with slug derived from `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`
- Example `add_memory` / `search_memory` flows including cross-project recall
- Fallback via the `schist-memory` CLI (`dist/cli/memory-cli.js`) with correct flag syntax
- Privacy notes: shared DB by default, no hub/spoke sync, append-only not enforced at the memory layer

## Why tag convention, not schema column
FTS5 already indexes the \`tags\` column, so \`project:<slug>\` in the tags array gives tag-scoped retrieval via the existing \`query\` field. If the convention proves noisy at scale, adding an indexed \`scope\` column later is a clean migration (parse \
items.author: yibeichan

items.title: fact_type description biased toward agent-as-narrator scenario, mislabels speaker turns in chat transcripts
items.content:
## Bug Description

The `fact_type` definitions in `fact_extraction.py` (Pydantic model descriptions and the user-message prompt) implicitly assume the speaker of the retained text is always the agent. When retain input is a multi-party chat transcript, user-spoken first-person facts *could* get mislabeled as `assistant` (→ `experience` in DB), effectively storing user experiences as if they were the agent's own. Affects the OpenClaw integration and any downstream that retains conversation transcripts.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Retain an OpenClaw-style transcript:
   ```
   [role: user]
   I bought a MacBook Air yesterday. What do you think?
   [user:end]

   [role: assistant]
   Nice choice — the M-series chips are great for...
   [assistant:end]
   ```
2. Inspect extracted facts.
3. The user's "I bought a MacBook Air yesterday" is classified as `assistant` (→ `experience`) instead of `world`.

## Expected Behavior

A first-person statement spoken by the user inside a transcript should be retained as a fact about the user, not as the agent's own experience.

## Actual Behavior

The current description —
```
'world' = objective/external facts.
'assistant' = first-person actions, experiences, or observations by the speaker.
```
— combined with the injected `Narrator: {agent_name} (AI agent — first-person statements like "I did X" are the agent's own actions; classify as "assistant")`, strongly steers the LLM to attribute any "I …" to the agent regardless of which role actually said it.

## Background

PR #775 broadened `fact_type` to fix misclassification of agent self-actions ("I changed X"), and PR #933 added the `narrator_section` hint for the same purpose. Both fixes are reasonable for the **agent-as-narrator** scenario (e.g., an agent autonomously logging its own tool calls), but they're applied unconditionally and may not fit the **transcript** scenario where the agent isn't the speaker of every "I …".

I don't have a strong opinion on the right fix — there ar

items.author: ringge

items.title: [提效] 方案2: 构建 TDD 驱动的 Skill 开发方法论
items.content:
# 背景

Superpowers 的 writing-skills skill 提出了一个核心洞察：**Skill 开发就是 TDD 应用到流程文档**。

流程：
1. **RED** - 先用 subagent 写压力测试场景，观察 agent 在没有 skill 的情况下如何失败
2. **GREEN** - 编写 skill 文档，使 agent 能通过测试
3. **REFACTOR** - 消除漏洞，保持测试通过

# 目标

为 OpenClaw 建立一套 TDD 驱动的 Skill 开发流程：

1. 每次写 Skill 前，先设计压力测试场景
2. 用 subagent 运行场景，记录 baseline 失败行为
3. 编写 Skill，让 agent 通过测试
4. 持续对抗性测试，发现新漏洞

# 子任务

- [ ] 编写 OpenClaw TDD Skill 开发指南
- [ ] 定义压力测试场景的标准格式
- [ ] 实现 skill 测试框架（subagent 运行场景 + 验证输出）
- [ ] 整理常见 agent rationalization 模式列表
- [ ] 在 Superpowers 测试框架基础上定制 OpenClaw 版本

# 参考资料

- Superpowers writing-skills: https://github.com/obra/superpowers/blob/main/skills/writing-skills/SKILL.md
- Superpowers testing-skills-with-subagents: https://github.com/obra/superpowers/tree/main/skills/writing-skills/testing-skills-with-subagents.md
items.author: ThomasLiu

items.title: [提效] 方案1: 引入 Superpowers 风格的两阶段 Subagent 评审机制
items.content:
# 背景

Superpowers 项目（obra/superpowers）实现了一套成熟的多 Agent 协作模式：

1. **Implementer Subagent** - 执行具体实现
2. **Spec Reviewer** - 验证实现是否符合需求规范
3. **Code Quality Reviewer** - 验证代码质量

每个任务完成后，先进行 Spec 合规性审查，再进行代码质量审查，两阶段缺一不可。

# 目标

在 AI Coding Fullstack 项目中引入这套两阶段评审机制：

1. 当 OpenClaw 执行实现任务时，自动调度 implementer subagent
2. implementer 完成后，触发 spec reviewer subagent 进行合规性检查
3. 通过后，触发 code quality reviewer subagent 进行质量检查
4. 两阶段都通过才标记任务完成

# 子任务

- [ ] 研究 Superpowers 的 subagent-driven-development skill 实现细节
- [ ] 设计 OpenClaw 的两阶段评审流程
- [ ] 实现 spec reviewer subagent 调度逻辑
- [ ] 实现 code quality reviewer subagent 调度逻辑
- [ ] 在 AGENTS.md 中定义评审规则
- [ ] 编写测试验证流程

# 参考资料

- Superpowers subagent-driven-development: https://github.com/obra/superpowers/blob/main/skills/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md
- Superpowers systematic-debugging: https://github.com/obra/superpowers/blob/main/skills/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md

items.title: chore: Configure Renovate
items.content:
Welcome to [Renovate](https://redirect.github.com/renovatebot/renovate)! This is an onboarding PR to help you understand and configure settings before regular Pull Requests begin.

🚦 To activate Renovate, merge this Pull Request. To disable Renovate, simply close this Pull Request unmerged.

📚 See our [Reading List](https://docs.renovatebot.com/reading-list/) for relevant documentation you may be interested in reading.

🔡 Do you want to change how Renovate upgrades your dependencies? Add your custom config to `renovate.json` in this branch. Renovate will update the Pull Request description the next time it runs.



---
### Detected Package Files

 * `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (github-actions)
 * `.github/workflows/publish.yml` (github-actions)
 * `package.json` (npm)
 * `renovate.json` (renovate-config-presets)

### Configuration Summary

Based on the default config's presets, Renovate will:

  - Start dependency updates only once this onboarding PR is merged
  - Hopefully safe environment variables to allow users to configure.
  - Show all Merge Confidence badges for pull requests.
  - Enable Renovate Dependency Dashboard creation.
  - Use semantic commit type `fix` for dependencies and `chore` for all others if semantic commits are in use.
  - Ignore `node_modules`, `bower_components`, `vendor` and various test/tests (except for nuget) directories.
  - Group known monorepo packages together.
  - Use curated list of recommended non-monorepo package groupings.
  - Show only the Age and Confidence Merge Confidence badges for pull requests.
  - Apply crowd-sourced package replacement rules.
  - Apply crowd-sourced workarounds for known problems with packages.
  - Ensure that every dependency pinned by digest and sourced from GitHub.com contains a link to the commit-to-commit diff
  - Correctly link to the source code for golang.org/x packages
  - Link to pkg.go.dev/... for golang.org/x packages' title
  - Pin Docker digests.
  - Pin `github-action` digests.
  - Enable

items.title: fix: Sửa logic skill execute-test-case-frontend — DOM snapshot + generate-then-execute [RD-1080]
items.content:
Jira issue: https://ignify-rd.atlassian.net/browse/RD-1080
Repository: https://github.com/ignify-rd/agent-skills

Summary: Sửa logic của skill excute-test-case-frontend

## Plan

## Mục tiêu

Sửa lại toàn bộ luồng thực thi của skill `execute-test-case-frontend` theo hướng **DOM Snapshot → Generate Script → Execute → Ghi kết quả Excel**, thay thế cách tiếp cận step-by-step MCP hiện tại.

---

## Thay đổi chính trong `SKILL.md`

### 1. Input (cập nhật)

| Tham số | Mô tả |
|---------|-------|
| `url` | URL trang web cần test (tham số cấp cao, bắt buộc) |
| `file` | Đường dẫn file Excel chứa test case (.xlsx) |

> URL không còn chỉ đọc từ cột D (precondition) — nay là tham số đầu vào riêng biệt. Cột D vẫn có thể chứa action phức tạp (login trước khi vào form, v.v.).

### 2. Luồng mới: Generate-then-Execute

**Bước 1 — Đọc test case từ Excel**
- Đọc sheet "TestCase" (hoặc sheet đầu tiên), lọc các row thỏa mãn **đồng thời**:
  - Cột G trống (chưa chạy).
  - Thuộc nhóm **"Kiểm tra validate"** (xác định qua tên Test Suite ở cột A hoặc nhãn loại test case trong dữ liệu). **Không thực thi các test case thuộc nhóm "Kiểm tra chức năng".**
- Nhóm các test case validate theo form/page (cột A — Test Suite).

**Bước 2 — Scan DOM để lấy cấu trúc form**
- Dùng `mcp__playwright__browser_navigate(url)` để mở trang.
- Dùng `mcp__playwright__browser_evaluate` để extract **Accessibility Tree + form structure** (chỉ giữ lại `form`, `input`, `select`, `button`, `[role=combobox]`, `[role=listbox]` cùng các attributes: `id`, `name`, `placeholder`, `aria-label`, `type`, `data-testid`).
- Kết quả là một JSON rút gọn mô tả các field có trên trang.

**Bước 3 — Generate Playwright script**
- Gửi JSON DOM + danh sách test case validate (title, steps mô tả ngôn ngữ tự nhiên, assertion) cho LLM.
- LLM sinh ra một đoạn code Playwright async duy nhất chạy toàn bộ các test case của nhóm đó:
  - Dùng locator chính xác từ DOM (ưu tiên `id` → `aria-label` → `placeholder` → `data-testid`).
  - Mỗi test cas

items.title: [Bug] MCP tools fail with login fail even when API key is valid
items.content:
## 问题描述

使用 minimax-coding-plan-mcp 时，web_search 和 understand_image 工具返回认证错误，但 API Key 实际上是有效的。

## 环境信息

- MCP Server: minimax-coding-plan-mcp
- Client: OpenClaw (使用 mcporter 调用)
- OS: macOS
- uvx 路径: 已确认可用

## 复现步骤

1. 配置 MCP server 使用 Token Plan API Key
2. 设置 MINIMAX_API_HOST=https://api.minimaxi.com (中国版)
3. 调用 web_search 或 understand_image 工具

## 错误信息

API Error: login fail: Please carry the API secret key in the Authorization field of the request header, please check your API key and API host.

## 额外发现

- 直接用 curl 调用 MiniMax API 可以成功（用同一个 API Key）
- MCP server 本身在线（mcporter list 显示 2 tools, healthy）
- 问题是 MCP server 内部调用 API 时认证失败

## 关联 Issue

- #981: 类似的认证错误问题
- #20: OpenClaw MCP 进程管理问题

## 可能原因

1. MCP server 内部调用 API 的方式与直接 API 调用不同
2. Token Plan 的 API 可能需要特殊的认证头或端点
3. 可能是 OpenClaw mcporter 与 minimax-coding-plan-mcp 之间的兼容性问题

---

注意：这是通过 OpenClaw 的 mcporter 工具调用的，不是直接通过 Claude Desktop 或 Cursor

items.title: release: v0.5.0
items.content:
## Release v0.5.0

All packages bumped to 0.5.0. Cross-references updated.

### What's new since 0.4.16
- **Identity & Auth:** Principal model, OAuth 2.1 (PKCE, DCR), XAA enterprise auth (Google/Azure/Okta)
- **Federation:** Hub-and-spoke sync, signed requests, pairing tokens, encrypted keystore, originator enforcement
- **Memory:** Temporal validity, relationships, predictive bootstrap, auto entity detection
- **Web Admin:** Server-rendered pages for principals, connectors, IdPs, memory, instance
- **MCP:** Structured errors with retry hints, 30s timeout, success echo, tags normalization
- **CLI:** `flair test`, init progress indicator, default soul entries, admin auth fallback
- **Tests:** 270 unit tests (OAuth, XAA, Credential, Relationship, Federation, CLI)
- **Docs:** Federation guide, auth/OAuth/XAA guide, updated README and CHANGELOG
- **Infrastructure:** Harper 5.0.0 stable

### Security
- Sherlock review: APPROVED (2026-04-16)
- 3 rounds of federation security fixes (signed sync, keystore, pairing tokens, originator enforcement, timestamp ceiling, fail-closed)
- Auth middleware: no more password caching, OAuth endpoints properly bypassed

### Packages
- `@tpsdev-ai/flair` 0.5.0
- `@tpsdev-ai/flair-client` 0.5.0
- `@tpsdev-ai/flair-mcp` 0.5.0
- `@tpsdev-ai/openclaw-flair` 0.5.0

items.author: buremba

items.title: Additional scan checks
items.content:
Added additional scan checks:

  - CHANNEL_DM_POLICY: Checks whether messaging DMs are open to anyone instead of restricted to trusted senders.
  - MCP_ENABLE_ALL_SERVERS: Checks whether all project MCP servers are automatically trusted.
  - MCP_FILESYSTEM_ROOTS: Checks whether an MCP filesystem server can access broad or sensitive folders.
  - MCP_SERVER_PINNING: Checks whether MCP server packages are unpinned or installed through risky shell commands.
  - MCP_REMOTE_TRANSPORT_AUTH: Checks whether remote MCP servers use unsafe HTTP or lack explicit auth headers.
  - INSTALLED_ARTIFACT_RISK: Checks installed plugins/extensions for risky install or execution patterns.
  - SKILL_PERMISSION_BOUNDARIES: Checks whether installed plugins/extensions declare overly broad permissions.
  - LOCAL_STATE_EXPOSURE: Checks persistent agent instruction files for plaintext secrets.
  - SKILL_EXTERNAL_ORIGIN: Checks whether installed plugins/extensions come from unpinned or mutable sources.
  - WORLD_WRITABLE_ARTIFACTS: Checks whether plugin or persistent-state files can be modified by other users.
  - PLUGIN_DEPENDENCY_PINNING: Checks whether installed plugins/extensions use unpinned dependency versions.
  - SENSITIVE_SCOPE_DECLARATIONS: Checks whether plugins declare sensitive access without matching ClawReins policy coverage.

These checks are not limited to the main OpenClaw config, so some additional discovery implementation for things like plugins and extensions were created.

## OpenClaw 项目深度报告

# OpenClaw 项目动态日报（2026-04-16）

---

## 1. 今日速览

OpenClaw 在过去24小时内保持极高活跃度，共处理 **500条 Issues 更新**（新开/活跃 436，已关闭 64）和 **500条 PR 更新**（待合并 357，已合并/关闭 143），显示出社区与开发团队的高度参与。项目于昨日发布 **v2026.4.15-beta.1** 版本，重点增强了模型认证状态的可视化监控能力。然而，多个用户报告了 **v2026.4.14 引入的严重回归性 Bug**，主要集中在安装配置阶段的 `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')` 崩溃问题，影响新用户 onboarding 体验。整体项目处于快速迭代但需警惕稳定性风险的状态。

---

## 2. 版本发布

### ✅ v2026.4.15-beta.1 正式发布  
**发布时间**：2026-04-15  
**GitHub Release**: [v2026.4.15-beta.1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.15-beta.1)

#### 主要更新内容：
- **Control UI / Overview 新增 Model Auth 状态卡片**：实时展示 OAuth Token 健康状态及提供商速率限制压力，支持过期/即将过期 Token 的视觉告警。
- **后端支持**：新增 `models.authStatus` 网关方法，自动剥离敏感凭证并缓存结果 60 秒，提升安全性与性能。

> ⚠️ **无破坏性变更**，但建议用户在 Control UI 中刷新页面以加载新组件。

---

## 3. 项目进展

今日合并/关闭的 PR 主要集中在 **Bug 修复、安全加固与测试覆盖增强**，推动项目向更稳定方向迈进：

| PR | 类型 | 说明 |

| [#66184](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66184) | 🧠 UX 优化 | 减少对话回复中过度确认性语言，提升 GPT-5 自主推进感与人格一致性。 |
