items.title: ci: implement optimized release pipeline
items.content:
## Description

Implement unified CI/CD release automation with semantic versioning across all three packages:
- **Python (headroom-ai)** — pip package on PyPI
- **TypeScript SDK (headroom-ai)** — npm package on npmjs.org
- **OpenClaw plugin (headroom-openclaw)** — npm package on npmjs.org and GitHub Package Registry

Currently the three packages are independently versioned (0.5.25 / 0.1.0 / 0.1.0). This PR introduces a single-source-of-truth version in `pyproject.toml` that propagates to all packages on every release, driven by conventional commit messages.

Fixes #(issue number)

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [x] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [x] Documentation update
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [x] Code refactoring (no functional changes)

## Changes Made

### New Files

**Scripts:**
- `scripts/version-sync.py` — Reads version from `pyproject.toml`, updates all 4 version files. Supports `--version X.Y.Z` and `--bump {major,minor,patch}`.
- `scripts/changelog-gen.py` — Parses conventional commits since last tag, groups by type, generates markdown changelog with breaking change detection.
- `scripts/verify-versions.py` — Pre-release sanity check that all 4 version files are in sync.
- `scripts/tests/test_version_sync.py` — 5 tests for version-sync.py
- `scripts/tests/test_changelog_gen.py` — 23 tests for changelog-gen.py

**Workflows:**
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — Unified release pipeline: detect → build → publish-pypi → publish-npm → publish-github-packages → create-release
- `.commitlintrc.json` — Conventional commit enforcement via `@commitlint/config-conventional`

**Local Testing (act):**
- `.actrc` — Default `act` flags (Ubuntu runner, reuse, quiet)
- `.github/act/dry-run.json` — `act` event file for dry-run testing
- `.github/act/push-feat.json` —
items.author: JerrettDavis

items.title: openclaw-mem0 declares kind: "memory" but does not register an active memory capability/runtime.
items.content:
openclaw-mem0 declares kind: "memory" but does not register an active memory capability/runtime.
On current OpenClaw it loads as:
Shape: non-capability
Capability mode: none
>
and with:
plugins.slots.memory = "openclaw-mem0"

OpenClaw reports:
No active memory plugin is registered for the current config

In other words, the plugin is loaded, but it never calls the memory-slot registration APIs, so it cannot become the active memory backend.

items.title: feat(plugin): enable onboarding from any channel
items.content:
## Summary

- **Plugin `dynamic-context.ts`**: 放宽 `before_prompt_build` 的 session gate — 当 working memory 中 `onboarding` section 存在时，任何 channel（Telegram/Discord/webchat）都注入 working memory，让 agent 能从任意渠道启动或继续 onboarding。Onboarding 完成（section 删除）后 gate 自动收窄，非 BotCord session 恢复零注入
- **`onboarding_instruction.md`（plugin + CLI）**: 独立的 onboarding 判定和执行文档，定义权威信号（`<section_onboarding>` 存在性）、基于 section 存在性的进度追踪表（STEP 1~5 各有锚点 section）、反例约束
- **SKILL.md Quick Entry 更新**（plugin + CLI）: 引用 `onboarding_instruction.md`

## Background

Instance 5 问题复现：用户通过 OpenClaw webchat 发 "ok connected"，sessionKey 不是 `botcord:owner:main` 也不是 room session → `buildDynamicContext` 直接 return null → `readOrSeedWorkingMemory` 从未执行 → onboarding 永远不触发。

根因：`before_prompt_build` hook 对所有 plugin 都触发（已通过阅读 OpenClaw 源码确认），但 `dynamic-context.ts` 自我门控过严。

## Test plan

- [x] `npm test` — 325 tests pass（含 2 个新增测试）
- [ ] 部署到测试实例，从 webchat 发消息验证 onboarding seed 注入
- [ ] 从 Telegram/Discord channel 验证 onboarding 注入
- [ ] 验证 onboarding 完成后非 BotCord session 不再注入（gate 收窄）

items.title: Feature Request: WorkBuddy Session Log Format Compatibility
items.content:
# Feature Request: WorkBuddy (Tencent Cloud) Session Log Format Compatibility

## Summary

Evolver currently supports OpenClaw session log format for session transcript analysis, but **WorkBuddy** (腾讯云 Coding Copilot / tencent-cloud.coding-copilot) uses a different conversation log format. When running Evolver on a WorkBuddy workspace, session logs are not detected (`session_logs_missing` signal), limiting Evolver's ability to learn from conversation context.

## Environment

- **Platform:** Windows 11 (win32)
- **Evolver version:** latest (installed 2026-04-16)
- **Node.js:** v24.14.1
- **Host IDE:** WorkBuddy (tencent-cloud.coding-copilot)
- **Workspace:** Standard WorkBuddy Claw workspace layout

## Current Behavior

When Evolver scans a WorkBuddy workspace:

1. ✅ Memory files (MEMORY.md, daily logs) are correctly detected and read
2. ❌ Session transcript logs are **not detected** — signal: `session_logs_missing`
3. Evolver reports: `[NO SESSION LOGS FOUND]`

WorkBuddy stores session history differently from OpenClaw:
- **OpenClaw format:** Session transcripts in `memory/` or workspace-specific paths
- **WorkBuddy format:** Conversation history managed internally by the IDE plugin, with working memory in `.workbuddy/memory/` (dated markdown files + MEMORY.md)

## Expected Behavior

Evolver should be able to parse WorkBuddy's working memory files (`.workbuddy/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`) as session context, since these daily logs contain:
- Task descriptions and outcomes
- Tool calls and results
- User preferences and corrections
- Error context and resolutions

This would give Evolver much richer signal data for evolution on WorkBuddy workspaces.

## Suggested Solution

Option A (Minimal): Allow Evolver to treat `.workbuddy/memory/*.md` files as session log sources when running on a WorkBuddy workspace.

Option B (Full): Add a `WORKBUDDY_MEMORY_DIR` env variable (similar to `MEMORY_DIR`) that points Evolver to WorkBuddy's memory directory, and add a WorkBuddy-specific

items.title: fix: pain ID propagation and ledger write for new principles (Issue #333)
items.content:
## Summary

Fixes GitHub Issue #333 — new pain signals could not flow through the compilation pipeline due to two gaps:

1. **Pain ID not propagated end-to-end**: `recordPainEvent()` return value was void; pain flag → evolution queue → `createPrincipleFromDiagnosis()` had no access to the real trajectory AUTOINCREMENT ID. Fixed across:
   - `trajectory.ts`: `recordPainEvent()` now returns `number` (the inserted row ID)
   - `pain.ts`: `buildPainFlag()` accepts `pain_event_id`, captured from `recordPainEvent()` return before writing flag
   - `queue-io.ts`: `RecentPainContext` carries `painEventId?: number`
   - `evolution-worker.ts`: all 3 pain flag parsing paths (KV contract, JSON, KV fallback) now parse and forward `painEventId`

2. **New principles invisible to compiler**: `createPrincipleFromDiagnosis()` wrote to `trainingStore` but NOT to `tree.principles` (the ledger the compiler reads via `loadLedger()`). Result: compiler always saw "no patterns" for any new principle.
   - `principle-tree-ledger.ts`: added `addPrincipleToLedger()` function
   - `evolution-reducer.ts`: `createPrincipleFromDiagnosis()` now calls `addPrincipleToLedger()` with proper field mapping (`trigger` → `triggerPattern`, `source.painId` → `derivedFromPainIds`, etc.)

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run test:unit` — 1840 tests pass
- [x] `npm run build` — TypeScript compiles clean
- [x] `sync-plugin.mjs` — plugin deployed to `~/.openclaw`
- [x] Existing P_066-P_071 "no patterns" expected for legacy data (their pain IDs don't match trajectory.db); new pain signals will work correctly

items.title: TypeError in formatDocsLink when channel plugin meta.docsPath is undefined during onboarding
items.content:
## Bug Description

During onboarding (`openclaw onboard`), selecting a configured channel and choosing "Skip (leave as-is)" causes a crash:

```
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `openclaw onboard` (quickstart flow)
2. Have a channel already configured (e.g., Feishu)
3. Select the configured channel
4. Choose "Skip (leave as-is)"
5. → Crash with TypeError

## Root Cause

In `src/terminal/links.ts`, the `formatDocsLink` function calls `path.trim()` without null-checking:

```typescript
function formatDocsLink(path, label, opts) {
    const trimmed = path.trim();  // ← crashes when path is undefined
    ...
}
```

This is called from `formatChannelSelectionLine` in the channel registry:

```typescript
function formatChannelSelectionLine(meta, docsLink) {
    const docs = meta.selectionDocsOmitLabel 
        ? docsLink(meta.docsPath)          // ← meta.docsPath can be undefined
        : docsLink(meta.docsPath, docsLabel);
    ...
}
```

After the channel selection loop ends, `setupChannels` iterates over **all** installed channel entries to build selection notes. Some channel plugins have `meta.docsPath` set to `undefined` (these same plugins also show as `undefined: undefined` in the Channel status output), which triggers the crash.

## Suggested Fix

```diff
function formatDocsLink(path, label, opts) {
-    const trimmed = path.trim();
+    const trimmed = (path ?? "").trim();
```

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: 2026.4.14
- OS: macOS (Darwin 25.3.0)
- Node.js runtime

items.title: Relax Telegram plugin schema to allow install before credential setup
items.content:
### Motivation
- OpenClaw validates plugin config at install time, and requiring `apiId`, `apiHash`, and `sessionString` in the plugin schema caused installation to fail before users could set credentials. 
- The `format: "password"` on `sessionString` triggered validator warnings in OpenClaw.

### Description
- Removed the top-level `required` list from `configSchema` in `openclaw.plugin.json` so installs do not fail when credentials are not yet configured. 
- Removed the unsupported `format: "password"` property from the `sessionString` schema entry to eliminate validator warnings. 
- Left runtime validation in `src/index.ts` intact so the channel still enforces presence of `apiId`, `apiHash`, and `sessionString` when starting.

### Testing
- Ran `npm test` and all tests passed (`43 pass`, `0 fail`).
- Ran `npm run lint` and it completed successfully (only an informational Biome schema-version message was reported).

------

items.content:

Only .ts files are provided, with no build step and no corresponding .js files.
OpenClaw fails silently on load (no logs, no errors).
It requires compilation via tsc or direct provision of .js files.

Issue 2: The README configuration example does not match configSchema
The README specifies "path": "...",

Configuration following the README will not take effect.
items.author: awecsfgvs

items.title: fix(memory-lancedb): reject inbound envelope metadata during capture and recall
items.content:
## Summary

Fix memory-lancedb prompt contamination by preventing OpenClaw transport/envelope metadata from being captured as long-term memory and later recalled into prompts.

## Root cause

`memory-lancedb` auto-capture was storing raw user message text from `agent_end`, including OpenClaw-injected inbound scaffolding such as:

- `Conversation info (untrusted metadata)`
- `Sender (untrusted metadata)`
- `Replied message (untrusted, for context)`
- `active-turn-recovery` boilerplate
- `[media attached: ...]` annotations

Those contaminated entries could later be recalled by `before_agent_start` and prepended into new prompts, which in a real traced failure poisoned a dispatched subagent run and caused it to work the wrong task.

## What changed

- add `looksLikeEnvelopeSludge(...)` to detect transport/meta contamination
- add `sanitizeForMemoryCapture(...)` to strip inbound metadata, timestamp prefixes, media annotations, and plugin scaffolding before capture
- reject contaminated candidates in `shouldCapture(...)`
- filter contaminated recall candidates before injecting prompt context
- add a defense-in-depth filter in `formatRelevantMemoriesContext(...)`

## Why this is safe

This is narrowly scoped to transport/envelope contamination. Legitimate preference/fact/entity capture and recall paths still work, but memories that look like OpenClaw metadata scaffolding are now excluded.

## Tests

Targeted extension-memory tests pass locally, including new regression coverage for:

- rejecting inbound envelope sludge during capture
- stripping contaminated content during sanitization
- suppressing contaminated memories during recall/context formatting
- preserving clean memories

items.title: fix: 安全的 legacy 升级流程 + 旧版 openclaw 兼容
items.content:
## Summary

- 重写升级流程为 4 场景分支（legacy / update / fresh / deadlock / broken），每个场景有独立的安全处理路径和失败回滚
- legacy 迁移采用 rename（非 delete）旧目录，安装失败时完整恢复
- deadlock 修复（channels.dmwork 存在但插件未装）：临时移除配置 → 安装 → 恢复，失败时抛错而非静默
- `pluginsInstall()` 三层参数降级：`--force --dangerously-force-unsafe-install` → `--force` → 裸 install，兼容所有版本 openclaw
- 所有配置写入改用 `writeConfigAtomic()`（tmp + rename），防止 gateway 读到截断 JSON
- doctor `--fix` 移除旧的破坏性 `cleanupLegacyPlugin()` 调用，改用场景感知的非破坏性清理
- CI tag 发布顺序修正：先 `npm version` 再 `npm run build`，确保 dist 版本号正确

## Test plan

- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` 编译通过
- [x] `npm test` 533 个测试全部通过
- [ ] 旧版 openclaw（无 --force）环境验证三层降级
- [ ] legacy 场景：extensions/dmwork 存在 → 迁移成功 → 旧目录被 rename
- [ ] deadlock 场景：channels.dmwork 存在但插件未装 → 自动修复
- [ ] broken 场景：stage 目录残留 → 自动清理后安装

items.author: caster-Q

items.title: fix(codex): auto-enable harness plugin for forced runtime
items.content:
## Summary
When `agents.defaults.embeddedHarness.runtime` is forced to `codex`, OpenClaw can start the Codex harness without activating the Codex plugin first. That leaves the runtime selected but its harness-owned plugin functionality unavailable at startup.

This PR closes that gap by treating configured embedded harness runtimes as plugin auto-enable signals and by letting plugin manifests declare ownership of specific agent harness runtimes.

## What changed
- add Codex plugin manifest activation metadata for the `codex` harness runtime
- detect configured embedded harness runtimes from defaults, per-agent config, and `OPENCLAW_AGENT_RUNTIME`
- auto-enable plugins whose manifests declare `activation.onAgentHarnesses`
- extend activation planning so harness-runtime triggers match manifest activation rules
- add coverage for manifest lookup, auto-enable resolution, channel plugin ids, and activation planning

## Files in scope
- `extensions/codex/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `src/config/plugin-auto-enable.core.test.ts`
- `src/config/plugin-auto-enable.shared.ts`
- `src/config/plugin-auto-enable.test-helpers.ts`
- `src/config/plugin-auto-enable.types.ts`
- `src/plugins/activation-planner.test.ts`
- `src/plugins/activation-planner.ts`
- `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts`
- `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.ts`
- `src/plugins/manifest.ts`

## Non-goals
- no changes to logging surface behavior
- no attempt to address the separate logging-surface issue diagnosed tonight
- no broader runtime-selection or startup-flow refactor beyond the existing branch contents

## Validation
- publication only in this step; no additional local code changes were made for this PR
- immediate CI status will be checked after PR creation

items.title: [Feature]: ：[Bug] image tool: URL images not converted to base64 before MiniMax VL API cal
items.content:
### Summary


**Environment**
- OpenClaw version: (check via `openclaw --version`)
- Node.js: v24.14.1
- OS: Windows_NT 10.0.19045
- Model: minimax/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed
- API key type: Coding Plan (`sk-cp-*`)

**Bug Description**
When using the `image` tool with an image URL (e.g., `https://httpbin.org/image/jpeg`), the image is NOT downloaded and converted to base64 before being sent to MiniMax's VL endpoint (`/v1/coding_plan/vlm`). Instead, the raw URL string is sent as the `image_url` field, causing the API to return:

```
{"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"invalid params, chat content is empty (2013)"}}
// or
{"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"invalid params, invalid image URL"}}
```

This happens because:
1. MiniMax's VL API server (at `api.minimaxi.com`) cannot access external URLs from its server-side network
2. OpenClaw should convert URL images to base64 data URLs before sending, but it does not do this for the `image` tool when given a URL parameter

**Steps to Reproduce**
1. Configure MiniMax as the image model provider with a Coding Plan API key
2. Set `agents.defaults.imageModel` to `minimax/MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`
3. Call the `image` tool with a URL:
   ```
   image(prompt="描述图片内容", image="https://httpbin.org/image/jpeg")
   ```
4. Observe the error in gateway logs: `"chat content is empty (2013)"`

**Expected Behavior**
The image tool should:
- Detect that the input is a URL
- Download the image bytes
- Convert to base64 data URL (e.g., `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQ...`)
- Send the data URL to MiniMax VL API

**Actual Behavior**
The raw URL string `"https://httpbin.org/image/jpeg"` is sent directly as `image_url`, which MiniMax's server cannot access.

**Root Cause Analysis**
- File (dist): `image-DQ3Bkt42.js` and `minimax-vlm-ONacdeuV.js`
- The `describeImagesWithMinimax()` function at `image-DQ3Bkt42.js:54` correctly expects `image.buffer` (Uint8Array) and converts to base64 data U

## 优化内容
### 1. 入口引导优化
- 新增Windows用户特别提示，明确告知新手优先选择WSL安装（稳定性和兼容性更好，是Windows平台首选）

### 2. Windows安装指南优化
- 开头增加WSL推荐提示与跳转链接，引导新手使用WSL安装
- 飞书配置升级为官方一键安装脚本，无需手动创建应用和配置权限，扫码即可完成对接
- 旧手动配置方式折叠，仅面向需要自定义的用户，降低新手操作门槛
- 增加飞书官方教程链接，保证内容及时性

### 3. WSL安装教程优化
- 修正「安OpenOpenClaw」错别字
- 安装完OpenClaw后新增「配置API与消息平台」步骤，流程更连贯
- 加入飞书官方一键安装脚本，安装完即可直接配置飞书
- 增加API配置教程跳转链接，减少用户查找成本

## 优化背景
根据流量统计，大部分用户访问本教程是为了查看Windows平台安装方式，新手对WSL没有概念，容易选择直接Windows安装遇到兼容性问题。本次优化重点引导新手使用WSL作为Windows平台首选安装方式，同时简化飞书对接流程，提升新手体验。

- [x] 命令可执行，无错误
- [x] 内容符合官方最新文档

items.title: fix(skills): prevent nested redirect when skills root already has skills
items.content:
## Summary
- **Problem:** When `~/.openclaw/skills/skills` subdirectory exists, the heuristic in `resolveNestedSkillsRoot` incorrectly redirected the skills root, causing skills in `~/.openclaw/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md` to be ignored.
- **Why it matters:** Users with a `skills/skills` subdirectory in their managed skills directory would lose access to all their installed skills without understanding why.
- **What changed:** Added early exit check in `resolveNestedSkillsRoot` to detect if `dir` is already a valid skills root (`dir/*/SKILL.md` exists, excluding `skills` subdirectory) before checking for nested redirect.
- **What did NOT change:** Repository-style skill trees (`repo/skills/*/SKILL.md`) continue to work correctly. The nested detection heuristic is still applied when appropriate.
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression
## Root Cause (if applicable)
- **Root cause:** The `resolveNestedSkillsRoot` function applied a heuristic to all skill directories unconditionally: if `dir/skills` subdirectory exists and contains `SKILL.md`, redirect to that nested path. This was intended for repository-style skill trees but incorrectly triggered for managed skills directories.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No check to verify if the original directory is already a valid skills root before applying the nested redirect heuristic.
- **Contributing context:** The heuristic was designed for the pattern `repo/skills/*/SKILL.md` where `repo` is not a skills root but `repo/skills` is. It didn't account for cases where `dir` is already a valid root an

items.title: fix(upgrade): pin base image digest and rebuild stale sandboxes
items.content:
## Summary
- Pins the sandbox base image to the sha256 digest from `blueprint.yaml` during Dockerfile patching so Docker must pull on cache miss instead of reusing a stale `:latest` tag
- Pre-pulls the digest-pinned image before sandbox creation for progress feedback and fail-fast on registry errors
- Adds `nemoclaw upgrade-sandboxes` command to detect and rebuild stale sandboxes after CLI upgrade (`--auto` for non-interactive, `--check` for report-only)
- Integrates `upgrade-sandboxes` into `install.sh` post-onboard flow so the upgrade path automatically catches stale sandboxes

## Test plan
- [x] 7 new unit tests for digest parsing, Dockerfile pinning, and pre-pull ordering (114/114 pass)
- [ ] Manual: upgrade from v0.0.11 → current, verify `nemoclaw upgrade-sandboxes` detects stale sandbox
- [ ] Manual: verify newly created sandboxes use the pinned digest image
- [ ] Manual: verify `--auto` flag rebuilds without prompting

Closes #1904

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Release Notes

* **New Features**
  * Automatic sandbox upgrade during installation when sandboxes are detected.
  * New `nemoclaw upgrade-sandboxes` command to upgrade existing sandboxes with version checking and progress reporting (supports `--check`, `--auto`, `--yes` options).
  * Base image digest pinning for reproducible sandbox builds.

* **Tests**
  * Added comprehensive test coverage for blueprint digest handling and sandbox upgrade workflows.

items.author: 0xalank

items.title: 📰 科研 & 技术热点日报 - 2026-04-16 上午
items.content:
# 🧠 科研 & 技术热点日报

日期：2026-04-16 上午
生成时间：2026/04/16 08:35

## 📝 今日总结

# 2026年4月16日 科研与技术热点新闻总结

## 1. 今日最重要的技术趋势与热点

**a) 智能体（Agent）的“元认知”困境与工程化加速**  
今日新闻呈现出智能体发展的两个鲜明侧面：一方面，学术研究对智能体的“自我监控”（如元认知、自我预测）能力提出质疑。arXiv论文《Self-Monitoring Benefits from Structural Integration》指出，在连续时间、多时间尺度的智能体环境中，简单添加元认知模块并未带来统计显著的性能提升，暗示当前“插件式”的自我监控设计可能存在结构性缺陷。另一方面，产业界正大力推动智能体的工程化落地：OpenAI发布了新版**Agents SDK**，引入原生沙箱执行和模型原生框架，旨在帮助开发者构建更安全、长期运行的智能体；Google研究院则推出了专门用于**学术工作流**的AI智能体，协助图表生成和同行评审，显示智能体正从通用概念向垂直领域深度渗透。

**b) RAG系统的安全性与效率成为焦点**  
检索增强生成（RAG）的研究重点正从基础性能转向**安全攻防**与**系统优化**。arXiv上关于《AdversarialCoT: Single-Document Retrieval Poisoning for LLM Reasoning》的研究揭示了一种新型攻击：仅需污染知识库中的**单个文档**，通过提取LLM的推理框架并迭代生成对抗性思维链，即可系统性地误导RAG系统的输出。这暴露了RAG在安全假设上的脆弱性。与此同时，另一篇论文《The Effect of Document Selection on Query-focused Text Analysis》则从效率角度出发，系统评估了七种文档选择策略，结论指出**语义检索或混合检索**在多数场景下已是足够高效的选择，无需过度复杂的策略，为工程实践提供了明确指导。

**c) 大模型评估从“结果正确”转向“过程可信”**  
《Filtered Reasoning Score: Evaluating Reasoning Quality on a Model's Most-Confident Traces》一文直指当前大模型评估的核心局限：**高准确率并不等同于优质的推理过程**。模型可能通过记忆、有缺陷的推理或过度优化获得正确答案。该研究提出基于模型“最自信推理轨迹”的评估新范式，旨在区分那些准确率相近但推理能力实质不同的模型。这标志着一个重要的范式转变——社区开始超越结果导向的评估，转而关注模型内部的**推理质量与可靠性**。

**d) 软件工程与系统架构的智能化与规模化挑战**  
在软件工程领域，LLM正被用于实现**自动化漏洞验证**。论文《AnyPoC: Universal Proof-of-Concept Test Generation for Scalable LLM-Based Bug Detection》试图解决LLM报bug后需人工验证的瓶颈，通过自动生成概念验证（PoC）测试用例来实现漏洞检测的端到端自动化。在系统层面，Uber分享了应对超大规模单体仓库（Monorepo）的实践《GitFarm: Git as a Service for Large-Scale Monorepos》，揭示了传统Git工作流在PB级代码库下的瓶颈，并提出了“Git即服务”的架构解决方案，以应对克隆、同步等操作带来的巨大基础设施开销。

## 2. 值得关注的研究方向与突破

- **科学发现的“局部最优”陷阱**：一篇颇具哲学与跨学科意义的论文《The Non-Optimality of Scientific Knowledge》将科学发现轨迹类比为机器学习中的**梯度下降**，提出科学知识体系在任一历史时刻都可能是受历史偶然性、认知路径依赖和制度锁定影响的**局部最优解**，而非全局最优。这为理解科学范式的演变提供了新颖的计算视角。
- **无需外部教师的自我蒸馏**：《Self-Distillation Zero: Self-Revision Turns Binary Rewards into Dense Supervision》提出了一种新方法，让单一模型同时扮演“生成者”和“修订者”两个角色，利用二进制奖励信号生成密集的令牌级监督，显著提升了训练样本效率，为在缺乏高质量演示数据的场景下进行模型后训练提供了新思路。
- **临床表格数据的模式泛化**：《Schema-Adaptive Tabular Representation Learn

items.title: [Idea] Real-Time Video Enhancement with Generative AI
items.content:
## Inspired by
- hacksider/Deep-Live-Cam (90.7k stars)
- ggml-org/llama.cpp (103.8k stars)
- firecrawl/firecrawl (109.6k stars)

## Problem
Video content creators and streamers face multiple challenges:
- Poor video quality from webcams or phone cameras
- Need for real-time background replacement during live streams
- Desires to enhance video quality (upscaling, denoising) without post-processing
- Privacy concerns in video calls and remote work

## Proposed Solution
Create a Real-Time Video Enhancement System (RTVES) that:

1. **Real-Time Background Replacement**
   - Remove or replace backgrounds during live video calls
   - Support custom backgrounds, blur effects, and transparent overlays
   - Works on consumer hardware (CPU and GPU)

2. **AI Video Enhancement**
   - Upscale low-resolution video in real-time (720p to 1080p, 4K)
   - Denoise and deblur video feeds
   - Adjust lighting and color automatically
   - Face enhancement and smoothing

3. **Privacy and Security Features**
   - Video blurring for sensitive content
   - Face de-identification for privacy
   - Virtual camera integration for all platforms (Zoom, Teams, Discord, OBS)

4. **Creative Effects**
   - Face tracking and AR filters
   - Avatar replacement with AI-generated characters
   - Real-time style transfer (artistic filters)
   - Motion graphics and overlays

## Technical Architecture

Core Components:
- **Video Input**: Webcam, screen capture, video file, network stream
- **Processing Pipeline**: GPU-accelerated inference with CUDA/Vulkan/Metal
- **AI Models**: Lightweight models for real-time performance
  - Background segmentation (DeepLabV3, MODNet)
  - Super-resolution (Real-ESRGAN, ESRGAN)
  - Face detection and landmarks (MediaPipe, RetinaFace)
  - Style transfer (FAST Style Transfer)
- **Virtual Camera**: Output as virtual webcam device
- **UI/Control**: Settings, presets, real-time controls

## Key Features

1. **Performance Optimized**
   - Runs at 30-60 FPS on consumer hardware

items.title: 💡 [Idea] AI Agent Multi-Platform Task Orchestration Framework
items.content:
## Inspired by
- openclaw/openclaw (358.1k ⭐)
- n8n-io/n8n (184.2k ⭐)
- Significant-Gravitas/AutoGPT (183.5k ⭐)

## Problem
Current AI agents are often limited to single platforms or require complex integration. Users need a unified framework that can orchestrate AI tasks across multiple platforms (desktop, mobile, web, IoT devices) seamlessly.

## Proposed Solution
Create a **Multi-Platform AI Agent Orchestration Framework** that:

### 1. Platform-Agentic Task Management
- Define tasks in a platform-agnostic format
- Automatically route tasks to the optimal platform/agent
- Support task delegation and handoff between platforms

### 2. Universal Agent API
- Standard interface for interacting with any AI agent
- Pluggable backend (OpenAI, Anthropic, local models via llama.cpp)
- Auto-select best model based on task complexity and cost

### 3. Workflow Engine Integration
- Visual workflow builder (inspired by n8n and langflow)
- Native AI capabilities for workflow automation
- Human-in-the-loop checkpoints for sensitive operations

### 4. Cross-Platform State Synchronization
- Shared context and memory across all agents
- Persistent task state that survives platform switches
- Conflict resolution for concurrent operations

## Technical Considerations
- Use TypeScript/JavaScript for maximum platform compatibility
- Leverage llama.cpp for local inference on resource-constrained devices
- WebSocket-based real-time communication
- Implement graceful degradation when platform APIs are unavailable

## Use Cases
- Start a task on desktop, continue on mobile
- Orchestrate home automation with voice + visual agents
- Multi-step research tasks spanning web search, document analysis, and synthesis
- Collaborative AI workflows between multiple users

## Related Projects
- browser-use: Web automation
- langflow: Agent building
- firecrawl: Web scraping for AI

## Difficulty Level
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (High - complex cross-platform coordination)

Unified AI orchestration could

items.author: kevinten10

items.title: [Bug]: Wix.com templates cannot be cloned — anti-scraping blocks headless browser rendering
items.content:
## Description

Wix.com website templates cannot be cloned because Wix employs aggressive anti-scraping measures that prevent headless browsers from rendering the page content. The clone-website skill relies on browser automation (Chrome MCP / headless Chrome) for reconnaissance, screenshot capture, and CSS extraction, but Wix blocks these entirely.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `/clone-website https://www.wix.com/website-template/view/html/wh-1098`
2. The browser navigates to the URL
3. Page renders as a blank/empty page — Wix's JS-based rendering pipeline detects headless Chrome and refuses to load content
4. No meaningful screenshots, CSS values, or content can be extracted

## Expected Behavior

The browser should be able to render the Wix template page and the skill should extract design tokens, assets, and content for cloning.

## Actual Behavior

- Headless Chrome (`google-chrome --headless --screenshot`) produces a blank white page or a Wix loading screen that never resolves
- `web_fetch` (HTML-to-markdown) returns only the template description metadata, not the actual page content — because the page is fully JS-rendered
- Direct preview URLs (e.g., `editor.wix.com/html/editor/web/renderer/render/document/wh-1098`) return 400 errors
- Even non-headless-flagged Chrome fails because Wix likely checks for automation signals beyond just the User-Agent

### What I tried:
- Headless Chrome with `--disable-gpu` and custom window sizes (1440x900, 1440x3000)
- Fetching the raw HTML via `web_fetch` — only returns SEO metadata, not rendered content
- Trying alternative Wix preview URLs — all blocked or return errors
- The Wix page works fine in a normal interactive browser session

## Workaround

The only viable workaround was to manually screenshot the target design in a regular browser, then use the screenshots as a design reference to manually rebuild the landing page in HTML/CSS — bypassing the automated clone pipeline entirely.

## Suggestion

It would be helpful

items.title: chore: land VM-local dev WIP before deploy hardening
items.content:
## Summary

Three changes that had been live-edited on the deploy VM at \`/home/node/openclaw/aries-app\` have been drifting against origin for several deploy cycles. Landing them now before **PR B** (incoming) makes the deploy workflow destructively sync the VM working tree to match origin on every deploy.

### Changes

- **\`scripts/automations/daily-standup.mjs\`** — Adds \`preflightOrExit\` integration, a \`writeJson\` helper, and standup id/title constants. Wires the standup run into the shared preflight-or-exit pattern used by sibling automations.
- **\`scripts/verify-canonical-workspace.mjs\`** — Adds a \`resolveCanonicalRoot()\` helper that lets the verifier honor \`ARIES_CANONICAL_REPO_ROOT\`, fall back to the default expected root when its required markers are present, and fall back to the git root otherwise. Keeps the existing comparison logic unchanged.
- **\`tests/workspace-verify.test.ts\`** (new, 36 lines) — Smoke test that runs \`verify-canonical-workspace.mjs\` as a subprocess and asserts it exits cleanly when the default expected root is valid. Uses the shared \`resolveProjectRoot\` helper.

## Context

This is one half of the fix for the recurring "can't pull / deploy blocked by dirty VM" issue. Follow-up **PR B** will:

1. Untrack \`data/runtime-error-incidents.json\`, \`data/feedback-processing-log.json\`, and \`data/nightly-build-log.json\` (three files that cron automations write in-place, which is the root cause of the drift) and gitignore them.
2. Replace the deploy workflow's stash / preserve-untracked / pop dance with a straight \`git reset --hard \$checkout_target && git clean -fd\`.

That change destroys any remaining uncommitted work on the VM, so this PR lands the dev WIP first to make sure nothing is lost.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI typecheck + lint
- [ ] \`tsx --test tests/workspace-verify.test.ts\`
- [ ] \`node scripts/verify-canonical-workspace.mjs\` still runs clean in the repo root

items.author: imshengli

items.title: Managed browser AI snapshot broken on macOS in 2026.4.14 while ARIA snapshot still works
items.content:
## Summary
On OpenClaw `2026.4.14` with the managed browser on macOS, `openclaw browser snapshot --format ai` failed even though the managed browser itself was up and basic browser features worked.

In the affected setup:
- `openclaw browser start` worked
- `openclaw browser open ...` worked
- `openclaw browser tabs` worked
- `openclaw browser snapshot --format aria` worked
- `openclaw browser snapshot --format ai` failed or hung

## What we observed
This did **not** appear to be a simple browser-startup failure.

The failure narrowed down to the hidden Playwright AI snapshot / CDP attach path used for the AI snapshot flow.

A few concrete observations from live diagnosis on macOS:
- ARIA snapshots returned normally
- browser status showed the managed browser on CDP port `18800`
- direct CDP HTTP endpoints on the browser port behaved like Chrome CDP endpoints
- the AI snapshot path either timed out or failed while trying to use the Playwright attach/snapshot mechanism
- in one failure mode, the wrong websocket path appeared to be used and the gateway rejected it with `invalid request frame`
- even direct Playwright `connectOverCDP(http://127.0.0.1:18800)` connected to the browser websocket and then stalled until timeout in this environment

So the practical breakage seems to be in the Playwright-over-CDP attach / `_snapshotForAI` path, not in the managed browser generally.

## Workaround that restored usability
As a live-install workaround, routing `format=ai` through the role-derived snapshot path instead of the broken Playwright `_snapshotForAI` path restored usable output.

That brought back agent-friendly structured snapshot output with refs, which was enough to make browser automation usable again.

## Why this may matter
For affected users, the browser may look mostly healthy while the most useful agent-facing snapshot mode is broken.

That means:
- screenshots may work
- ARIA snapshots may work
- tabs/open/navigate may work
- but `format=ai` and some ref-driv

items.title: Add hermes-agent as a supported harness
items.content:
## Proposal

Add [hermes-agent](https://github.com/nousresearch/hermes-agent) (Nous Research, MIT, ~57k stars, released Feb 2026) as a first-class harness alongside the existing `openclaw` and `opencode` entries.

## Why

- Fastest-growing open-source agent framework of 2026 — meaningful population of real users will want to benchmark it.
- Ships browser automation natively (40+ built-in tools including web search, clicking, typing, screenshots, accessibility-tree snapshots) — a direct fit for ClawBench's 144 live-site task suite.
- Officially partnered with Browser Use (2026-04-09) as the default cloud-browser entry point — out-of-the-box compatibility with the CDP transport we already use.
- MIT-licensed and fully local (no telemetry) — no legal/privacy blockers to running it inside the container.

Covering Hermes next to `openclaw` and `opencode` gives the leaderboard a meaningful three-way comparison across Anthropic-native, generic-agent-framework, and self-improving-agent approaches.

## Scope

Follow the existing harness pattern. Concretely:

1. **New Docker layer** — `src/clawbench/data/docker/Dockerfile.hermes` mirroring `Dockerfile.openclaw` / `Dockerfile.opencode`, built on `Dockerfile.base`.
2. **Setup script** — `src/clawbench/data/docker/setup-hermes.sh` that runs the single-command installer from hermes-agent.org.
3. **Run script** — `src/clawbench/data/docker/run-hermes.sh` that invokes the Hermes CLI with the task `instruction` and wires stdout into the existing agent-message recording layer.
4. **Register the harness** — `src/clawbench/run.py:31` extend `HARNESSES = ("openclaw", "opencode", "hermes")` and `src/clawbench/run.py:352-353` add the file-triplet mapping.
5. **TUI choice** — `src/clawbench/tui.py:444` / `tui.py:510` already read from `HARNESSES` indirectly; confirm the new entry surfaces in the harness-picker.
6. **Docs** — `README.md` Harness table, `docs/integrations.md` if applicable.

## Open questions

- Hermes exposes a persistent-m

items.title: Feature Request: Session Metadata Queries (filter by age/size/agent)
items.content:
## Summary

Extend `openclaw sessions` to support filtering by agent, age, and size — and expose structured output for automation.

## Use Case

Fleet operators need to:
- List all sessions for a specific agent
- Find sessions older than N hours (stale/orphan candidates)
- Identify sessions exceeding size thresholds (context bloat)
- Get JSON output for scripting

## Proposed Interface

```bash
# Filter by agent
openclaw sessions --agent infra-lead

# Filter by age
openclaw sessions --older-than 24h
openclaw sessions --newer-than 1h

# Filter by size
openclaw sessions --larger-than 100KB

# Structured output
openclaw sessions --agent cto --json

# Combine filters
openclaw sessions --agent fleet-auditor --older-than 12h --json
```

## Current State

`openclaw sessions` provides a summary view but limited filtering. Cross-referencing session state with external work tracking (Linear, GitHub) requires manual correlation.

## Context

Part of a session lifecycle management initiative. Related requests:
- Session close API (#67368)
- Event-driven cleanup hooks

---
*Filed by Pescao Fleet CTO*

items.title: [Bug]: Auto-compaction (threshold-based) never fires in embedded runner
items.content:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Pi's threshold-based auto-compaction never fires in the embedded runner. Sessions grow past the configured trigger without compacting. Only manual `/compact` works. Confirmed by session JSONL — all compaction entries are manual, none are threshold-triggered.

### Steps to reproduce

  1. Set `agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokens`: 930000 with `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` and context1m: true (1,048,576 token window, threshold = ~118k tokens).
  2. Chat until `totalTokens` in the session transcript exceeds 118k.
  3. Continue chatting. Observe no auto-compaction fires.
  4. Verify in`~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/<id>.jsonl`: compaction records are only present after manual `/compact` commands.

### Expected behavior

Auto-compaction fires when `contextTokens` > `contextWindow` − `reserveTokens` (118,576 tokens), per `shouldCompact()` in `pi-coding-agent`.

### Actual behavior

Session reached 163k tokens (45k over threshold) across 14+ turns with no auto-compaction. All 3 compaction entries in the session JSONL were manual /compact calls.

### OpenClaw version

2026.4.14

### Operating system

Ubuntu 22.04

### Install method

sudo npm install -g openclaw@latest (system-level systemd service

### Model

anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6

### Provider / routing chain

openclaw → anthropic

### Additional provider/model setup details

`context1m: true` under `agents.defaults.models["anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"].params`. 
Context engine: legacy (default). Sandbox mode: all. Channel: Telegram.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
# Session JSONL — all compactions are manual, none threshold-triggered:
  type=compaction ts=2026-04-15T19:18:15Z tokensBefore=253640
  type=compaction ts=2026-04-15T23:02:09Z tokensBefore=163777
  type=compaction ts=2026-04-15T23:53:17Z tokensBefore=103206

  # totalTokens trajectory before compaction #2 — 14+ turns above 1
items.author: natechicago

items.title: fix(inter-session-completion-delivery): ensure completion delivery on ANNOUNCE_SKIP
items.content:
## Problem
Inter-session completion delivery could become unreliable when the announce path produced `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`.

In that path, the announce result could suppress the visible completion flow too early, which meant the completion toward `REPLY_TO_SESSION` was no longer deterministic. The practical effect was that a task could finish correctly but still fail to surface the final delivery reliably to the intended external session.

## What this PR changes
This PR makes completion delivery continue even when the announce path returns `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`.

Concretely, it:
- removes the early-stop behavior tied to `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` in the completion-delivery path
- preserves the established external route during inter-session completion delivery
- updates the announce-related tests to reflect the intended behavior

The key semantic change is that `ANNOUNCE_SKIP` suppresses the announce step, but it no longer suppresses the actual completion delivery toward `REPLY_TO_SESSION`.

## Why this is the correct behavior
The announce path is soft and LLM-mediated. Completion delivery is not.

Those two concerns should not be coupled. If the announce layer decides to skip, that may be acceptable for the announce itself, but it should not prevent a finished task from delivering its final completion to the target session.

This PR restores that separation by making the completion path deterministic even when the announce path is skipped.

## Files changed
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts`

## Tests and CI notes
The branch also received a follow-up CI-only fix after initial PR submission:
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts` — added the missing `gatewayCall` import to resolve `TS2304`
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts` — updated stale assertions to match current final `NO_REPLY` delivery behavior

After that follow-up, the remaining red check was narrowed

items.title: fix(agents): add compat.supportsPromptCacheKey to opt out of prompt_cache_key strip
items.content:
> AI-assisted (Claude Opus 4.6), driven by a maintainer. Fully tested — see Human Verification and the AI-assisted disclosure section below.

## Summary

- Problem: the strip added in #49877 (fixing #48155 — Volcano Engine DeepSeek rejects `prompt_cache_key` with HTTP 400) applies to every non-native baseUrl via the `usesExplicitProxyLikeEndpoint` gate, including OpenAI-compatible proxies that DO forward the field correctly (CLIProxy, LiteLLM, vLLM). Agents routed through those proxies get zero prompt caching as a side effect.
- Why it matters: operators running agents through an OpenAI-compatible proxy see `cached_tokens: 0` on every turn even with stable sessions and large repeating prefixes. On a ~40K-token prefix, that's ~40K uncached input tokens re-sent on each turn — wasted latency and wasted spend (or quota pressure on subscription-backed routes like Codex Plus).
- What changed: add `compat.supportsPromptCacheKey?: boolean` to `ModelCompatConfig` and extend `resolveProviderRequestCapabilities` to let operators override the strip per model. `undefined` preserves the existing default so #48155 continues to work without any config change. `true` opts out of the strip for proxies known to be compatible. `false` forces the strip for endpoints known to reject the field, even on native baseUrls.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default behavior is unchanged for every existing config; prompt caching on direct OpenAI / Azure OpenAI / OpenAI Codex native endpoints is unchanged; Anthropic `cacheRetention` is in a different code path and unaffected; the `createOpenAIResponsesContextManagementWrapper` in `openai-stream-wrappers.ts` is not modified — the new flag plugs into the already-computed `shouldStripResponsesPromptCache` capability.

- [x] API / contracts

## Trend Hunter: 2026 AI Agent Infrastructure Trend Raporu

**Araştırma Tarihi:** 2026-04-16
**Araştıran:** Trend Hunter Subagent
**Repo:** vopsiton/riks-context-engine
**Issue:** #22

---

## 1. Tool Calling / Function Calling (2026)

### Durum: Olgunlaşan Standart

Tool calling artık LLM'lerde first-class citizen. Tüm major provider'lar destekliyor:

| Provider | Adi | Durum |
|----------|-----|-------|
| **OpenAI** | Function Calling / Tool Use | GA, JSON Schema zorunlu |
| **Anthropic** | Tool Use | GA, 2025'te stable |
| **Google Gemini** | Function Calling | GA, multimodal |
| **Meta Llama** | Function Calling | Open source |
| **Mistral** | Function Calling | GA |

### 2026 Trendleri

- **Structured Output + Tool Use birlesmesi** - Tool calling artik sadece "bu fonksiyonu cagır" degil, complex nested tool execution
- **Parallel Tool Calls** - Ayni anda birden fazla tool cagırabilme (OpenAI ve Gemini destekliyor)
- **Tool Choice Control** - Model'in hangi tool'u sececeginde kisitlamalar (forced tools, forbidden tools)
- **Cross-model Portable Tool Schemas** - MCP ile standartlasma basladi

### Ongoru (2026+)

Tool calling'in gelecegi **MCP** ile kesisiyor. Function calling = "nasil cagıracağım", MCP = "neye cagıracağım" sorusunu cozyor. 2026'da tool discovery + tool execution birlesecek.

items.author: hughdidit

items.title: Fix load-time await usage in OpenClaw plugin and MCP server
items.content:
## Summary
- remove load-time top-level `await` from the OpenClaw plugin import path
- wrap MCP entrypoints in explicit `main()` functions instead of relying on top-level `await`
- regenerate the bundled MCP server output

## Why
OpenClaw was failing to load the linked plugin with:

```text
ReferenceError: await is not defined
```

The root cause was `lib/cli-runner.js` using a top-level dynamic import for `child_process`:

```js
const cp = await import(`node:${_cpmod}`)
```

That made module loading depend on host support for top-level await during plugin evaluation.

## Testing
- `npm test` in `mcp-server` (`44 passed`)
- verified the OpenClaw plugin now loads successfully after `openclaw plugins install -l ./openclaw` and `openclaw gateway restart`

items.author: 19Chris19

items.title: Cron job result serialization fails on special characters in edit tool arguments
items.content:
## Bug Description

Cron job `daily-memory-review` reports `Expected ',' or '}' after property value in JSON at position 492 (line 1 column 493)` even though the agent task completes successfully.

## Reproduction

1. Set up a cron job with `sessionTarget: isolated` and `delivery.mode: announce`
2. The agent uses the `edit` tool on a markdown file containing:
   - Chinese quotation marks (`...`)
   - Markdown bold syntax (`**...**`)
   - Mixed CJK + ASCII content
3. The agent completes all operations successfully (files written, delivery sent)
4. The cron framework marks the job as `error` with the JSON parse error

## Expected Behavior

Job should be marked as `ok` since the agent completed successfully and delivery was sent.

## Actual Behavior

- `lastRunStatus: "error"`
- `lastError: "Expected ',' or '}' after property value in JSON at position 492 (line 1 column 493)"`
- `lastDeliveryStatus: "delivered"` (delivery actually succeeded)
- `consecutiveErrors: 1`

## Details

The error appears to occur in the cron framework's post-processing / result serialization step, not during agent execution. The JSON parse error at position 492 on line 1 suggests a single-line JSON string is being parsed and failing on special characters from the `edit` tool's `oldText`/`newText` arguments.

## Environment

- OpenClaw version: latest (as of 2026-04-16)
- OS: Ubuntu Linux 6.8.0
- Node: v22.22.1
- Cron job config: `agentTurn` payload, `isolated` session, `announce` delivery to Telegram

## Cron Job State (from jobs.json)

```json
{
  "id": "621e8e50-da09-4ac1-bb47-8f1428af832a",
  "name": "daily-memory-review",
  "lastRunStatus": "error",
  "lastDurationMs": 120311,
  "lastDeliveryStatus": "delivered",
  "consecutiveErrors": 1,
  "lastError": "Expected ',' or '}' after property value in JSON at position 492 (line 1 column 493)"
}
```

items.title: Add OpenClaw “Goddess” Node agent cookbook with archive.org retrieval and memory tools
items.content:
### Motivation

- Provide a ready-to-use Node cookbook demonstrating a persona-driven agent that combines tool use, short-term memory, and external retrieval from `archive.org` for grounded occult/historical references. 
- Show a safe-by-design pattern with an explicit `cosmicConstitution` policy and constrained memory/tool interfaces to reduce fabrication and harmful outputs. 
- Offer a practical example developers can adapt for production, including notes for persistent memory, citation verification, and policy checks. 

### Description

- Adds a new cookbook page `content/cookbook/05-node/90-openclaw-goddess-agent.mdx` containing a full example and code sample for `openclaw-goddess-agent.ts`. 
- Implements an in-memory `MemoryEntry` store and two tools: `rememberInsight` to persist compact lessons and `recallInsights` to retrieve relevant memories. 
- Adds a `fetchOccultWisdomFromArchive` tool that queries `archive.org` advanced search and returns bibliographic leads with `archiveUrl` links. 
- Exports `runOpenClawGoddess(userPrompt)` which composes system instructions, recent memory context, and the three tools when calling `generateText` with the `openai('gpt-4o-mini')` model. 

### Testing

- No automated tests were added or executed as part of this change. 
- A developer-facing install instruction `pnpm add ai @ai-sdk/openai zod` is included for manual verification. 
- Recommend adding unit tests for tool parameter validation and a mock HTTP test for `fetchOccultWisdomFromArchive` in CI before production use.

items.title: ⚡ Bolt: optimize MMR re-ranking complexity from O(N^3) to O(N^2)
items.content:
💡 What: Optimized the MMR re-ranking algorithm from $O(N^3)$ to $O(N^2)$ by caching maximum similarity and updating it incrementally.
🎯 Why: The previous implementation was a significant performance bottleneck for large result sets.
📊 Impact: Reduced execution time for 500 items from ~6.8 seconds to ~57ms.
🔬 Measurement: Verified with a benchmark script and existing unit tests.

items.title: [Release] OpenClaw 2026.4.15-beta.1
items.content:
### Changes

- Control UI/Overview: add a Model Auth status card showing OAuth token health and provider rate-limit pressure at a glance, with attention callouts when OAuth tokens are expiring or expired. Backed by a new `models.authStatus` gateway method that strips credentials and caches for 60s. (#66211) Thanks @omarshahine.
- Memory/LanceDB: add cloud storage support to `memory-lancedb` so durable memory indexes can run on remote object storage instead of local disk only. (#63502) Thanks @rugvedS07.
- GitHub Copilot/memory search: add a GitHub Copilot embedding provider for memory search, and expose a dedicated Copilot embedding host helper so plugins can reuse the transport while honoring remote overrides, token refresh, and safer payload validation. (#61718) Thanks @feiskyer and @vincentkoc.
- Agents/local models: add experimental `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: true` to drop heavyweight default tools like `browser`, `cron`, and `message`, reducing prompt size for weaker local-model setups without changing the normal path. (#66495) Thanks @ImLukeF.
- Packaging/plugins: localize bundled plugin runtime deps to their owning extensions, trim the published docs payload, and tighten install/package-manager guardrails so published builds stay leaner and core stops carrying extension-owned runtime baggage. (#67099) Thanks @vincentkoc.
- QA/Matrix: split Matrix live QA into a source-linked `qa-matrix` runner and keep repo-private `qa-*` surfaces out of packaged and published builds. (#66723) Thanks @gumadeiras.
- Docs/showcase: add a scannable hero, complete section jump links, and a responsive video grid for community examples. (#48493) Thanks @jchopard69.

### Fixes

- Security/approvals: redact secrets in exec approval prompts so inline approval review can no longer leak credential material in rendered prompt content. (#61077, #64790)
- CLI/configure: re-read the persisted config hash after writes so config updates stop failing with stale-hash races. (
